# Noisemaker AI development contract

This document is an executable-source companion for an agent that must inspect,
author, validate, and diagnose Noisemaker programs and effects without guessing.
It describes current behavior, not intended behavior.

Source snapshots used for this contract:

- Noisemaker: `755071128ba6112753b9e976dc0ebbb8e55449b5`.
- Shade MCP: `7fd0d9758e64ec587950a49496b31eb59ae17857`.
- Shade MCP protocol: server name `shade-mcp`, server version `0.1.0`, protocol
  `2025-06-18`, with 18 tools returned by `tools/list`.
- The repository config runs `npx -y github:noisedeck/shade-mcp`; it does not pin
  that commit. Re-resolve and re-capture tool contracts before relying on this
  snapshot after dependency installation changes. See GAP-013.

Primary source roots:

- DSL: `shaders/src/lang/`.
- definition and graph runtime: `shaders/src/runtime/`.
- effect loading and DSL registration: `shaders/src/renderer/canvas.js`.
- backend behavior: `shaders/src/runtime/backends/webgl2.js` and `webgpu.js`.
- repository validation policy: `shaders/tests/test-harness.js`.
- Shade MCP source for the pinned snapshot:
  `https://github.com/noisedeck/shade-mcp/tree/7fd0d9758e64ec587950a49496b31eb59ae17857/src`.

## Reading the contract

Every field or behavior has one of these statuses:

- **enforced**: current validation rejects an illegal value.
- **consumed**: runtime code reads the value and changes behavior, but validation
  may be partial.
- **observed**: checked-in definitions contain the value; this does not imply the
  runtime consumes it.
- **unsupported**: there is no current authoring/query contract. It is a gap.

An omitted JavaScript property is `undefined`. JSON cannot represent functions,
`Map`, `Set`, class instances, `undefined`, or cyclic state; JSON shapes below are
the observable projections of those runtime values.

Common structural types:

```ts
type BackendName = "webgl2" | "webgpu"
type NonNegativeInteger = number // semantic predicate: integer && value >= 0
type FiniteNumber = number       // semantic predicate: Number.isFinite(value)
type Vec2 = [number, number]
type Vec3 = [number, number, number]
type Vec4 = [number, number, number, number]
type JsonPrimitive = string | number | boolean | null
type JsonValue = JsonPrimitive | JsonValue[] | { [key: string]: JsonValue }

type Diagnostic = {
  code: "S001" | "S002" | "S003" | "S004" | "S005" | "S006" |
        "S007" | "S008"
  message: string
  severity: "error" | "warning"
  nodeId?: string | number
  location?: { line: number, column: number | undefined }
  identifier?: string
}

type CompilationFailure = {
  code: "ERR_COMPILATION_FAILED"
  diagnostics: Diagnostic[]
}

type ExpansionFailure = {
  code: "ERR_EXPANSION_FAILED"
  errors: Array<{ message: string, step?: CompiledStep }>
}
```

`Diagnostic.location.column` is written from `node.loc.column`, while parser
nodes store `loc.col`; it can therefore be `undefined`. This is GAP-002.

## Runtime overview

The browser integration path is:

1. `CanvasRenderer.loadManifest()` discovers effect assets.
2. `CanvasRenderer.loadEffect()` imports `definition.js`, loads shader source,
   registers the effect and its DSL operator, and registers starter state.
3. `CanvasRenderer.compile(source)` calls `compileGraph(source)`.
4. `compileGraph()` lexes/parses/validates, expands effect steps into passes,
   computes a liveness allocation map, and returns a graph.
5. `Pipeline.compilePrograms()` compiles every referenced program for the active
   backend.
6. `Pipeline.render(time)` executes expanded passes in array order, presents the
   selected surface, then advances double-buffer bindings.

Sources: `shaders/src/renderer/canvas.js` — `loadEffectShaders()`,
`registerEffectWithRuntime()`; `shaders/src/runtime/compiler.js` —
`compileGraph()`; `shaders/src/runtime/pipeline.js` — `compilePrograms()`,
`render()`.

<!-- SURFACE:DSL -->
## DSL program

### Introspect

Legal namespaces are the current `VALID_NAMESPACES` set in
`shaders/src/runtime/tags.js`. Loaded operators are queryable through the
exported `ops` object in `shaders/src/lang/ops.js`. A loaded effect definition is
queryable with `getEffect(name)` or `getAllEffects()` from
`shaders/src/runtime/registry.js`.

The parse/compile projections are:

```ts
type Token = { type: string, lexeme: string, line: number, col: number }

type ParsedProgram = {
  type: "Program"
  plans: ParsedStatement[]
  vars?: Array<{ type: "VarAssign", name: string, expr: ParsedExpr }>
  render: { type: "OutputRef", name: string } | null
  namespace: {
    imports: Array<{ name: string, source: "search", explicit: true }>
    default: { name: string, source: "search", explicit: true }
    searchOrder: string[]
  }
  trailingComments?: string[]
}

type SurfaceValue =
  | { kind: "output", name: string }
  | { kind: "source", name: string }
  | { kind: "xyz", name: string }
  | { kind: "vel", name: string }
  | { kind: "rgba", name: string }
  | { kind: "mesh", name: string }
  | { kind: "state", name: "time" | "frame" | "mouse" | "resolution" |
                             "seed" | "a" }
  | { kind: "vol", name: string }
  | { kind: "geo", name: string }
  | { kind: "pipeline", name: string }
  | { kind: "temp", index: number }

type CompiledStep = {
  op: string
  args: Record<string, unknown>
  from: number | null
  temp: number
  builtin?: boolean
  namespace?: object
  rawKwargs?: Record<string, ParsedExpr>
  argSources?: Record<string, "array">
  leadingComments?: string[]
}

type CompiledPlan = {
  chain: CompiledStep[]
  write: { kind: "output", name: string } | null
  write3d: {
    tex3d: { kind: "vol", name: string }
    geo: { kind: "geo", name: string }
  } | null
  final: number | null
  states: object[]
  leadingComments?: string[]
}

type CompiledProgram = {
  plans: Array<CompiledPlan | CompiledBranch | { type: "Break" | "Continue" } |
               { type: "Return", value?: ParsedExpr }>
  diagnostics: Diagnostic[]
  render: string | null
  vars: ParsedProgram["vars"]
  searchNamespaces: string[]
  trailingComments?: string[]
}
```

Surface tokens and actual allocation behavior:

| Token family | Parser token | Read/write role | Preallocated state |
|---|---|---|---|
| `oN` | `OUTPUT_REF` | `read`, surface arg, `write`, `render` | `o0`–`o7`, double buffered |
| `sN` | `SOURCE_REF` | `read`, surface arg | not initially preallocated; graph references create a dynamic global surface |
| `volN` | `VOL_REF` | volume arg or `read3d`; `write3d` texture | `vol0`–`vol7` as 64×4096 2D atlases |
| `geoN` | `GEO_REF` | geometry arg or `read3d`; `write3d` geometry | `geo0`–`geo7`, screen-sized RGBA16F |
| `xyzN` | `XYZ_REF` | `read`, surface arg, `write` | dynamic global state surface when referenced |
| `velN` | `VEL_REF` | `read`, surface arg, `write` | dynamic global state surface when referenced |
| `rgbaN` | `RGBA_REF` | `read`, surface arg, `write` | dynamic global state surface when referenced |
| `meshN` | `MESH_REF` | `read`, surface arg, `write` | `mesh0`–`mesh7`; positions/normals/UV triplet, 256×256 defaults |

The lexer accepts one or more digits for every family. Only volume and geometry
parameter resolution explicitly checks `0` through `7`; `o8`, `o99`, and
`read(o8)` pass lexing and surface conversion. Do not infer an enforced `o0`–
`o7` range from the public model. See GAP-001.

Current connections are observable after compilation in
`graph.passes[].inputs`, `graph.passes[].outputs`, `graph.renderSurface`, and at
runtime in `pipeline.surfaces`, `pipeline.frameReadTextures`, and
`pipeline.frameWriteTextures`.

Sources: `shaders/src/lang/lexer.js` — `lex()`;
`shaders/src/lang/parser.js` — `parse()`;
`shaders/src/lang/validator.js` — `validate()`, `toSurface()`;
`shaders/src/runtime/pipeline.js` — `createSurfaces()`, `getFrameState()`.

### Act

The implemented grammar is below. It supersedes the optional-search grammar in
the comment at the top of `parser.js`; `search` is mandatory in code.

```ebnf
Program        ::= Comments SearchDirective Semicolons
                   { Comments Statement Semicolons }
                   [ Comments RenderDirective Semicolons Comments ] EOF
SearchDirective::= "search" Namespace { "," Namespace }
Statement      ::= VarAssign | ChainStmt | IfStmt | BreakStmt |
                   ContinueStmt | ReturnStmt
VarAssign      ::= "let" Identifier "=" Expr
IfStmt         ::= "if" "(" Expr ")" Block
                   { "elif" "(" Expr ")" Block }
                   [ "else" Block ]
Block          ::= "{" { Statement Semicolons } "}"
BreakStmt      ::= "break"
ContinueStmt   ::= "continue"
ReturnStmt     ::= "return" [ Expr ]
ChainStmt      ::= Chain                         (* validator requires terminal write *)
Chain          ::= ChainHead { "." ChainElement }
ChainHead      ::= Call | ReadCall | Read3DCall
ChainElement   ::= Call | WriteCall | Write3DCall | Subchain
Call           ::= Identifier "(" [ ArgList ] ")"
ArgList        ::= PositionalArgs | KeywordArgs
PositionalArgs ::= Expr { "," Expr } [ "," ]
KeywordArgs    ::= Identifier ":" Expr
                   { "," Identifier ":" Expr } [ "," ]
ReadCall       ::= "read" "(" SurfaceExpr ")"
Read3DCall     ::= "read3d" "(" VolumeExpr [ "," GeometryExpr ] ")"
WriteCall      ::= "write" "(" WriteTarget ")"
WriteTarget    ::= OutputRef | XyzRef | VelRef | RgbaRef | MeshRef | "none"
Write3DCall    ::= "write3d" "(" (Identifier | OutputRef | VolRef) ","
                   (Identifier | OutputRef | GeoRef) ")"
RenderDirective::= "render" "(" OutputRef ")"
Subchain       ::= "subchain" "(" SubchainArgs ")" "{"
                   "." Call { "." Call } "}"
SubchainArgs   ::= String | [ "name" ":" String [ "," "id" ":" String ] ]

Expr           ::= Additive
Additive       ::= Multiplicative { ("+" | "-") Multiplicative }
Multiplicative ::= Unary { ("*" | "/") Unary }
Unary          ::= [ "+" | "-" ] Primary
Primary        ::= Number | String | TripleString | Boolean | HexColor |
                   Array | ArrowFunction | "Math.PI" | Member | Identifier |
                   SurfaceRef | Call | Chain | "(" Expr ")"
Array          ::= "[" [ Expr { "," Expr } ] "]"
Member         ::= Identifier "." MemberSegment { "." MemberSegment }
HexColor       ::= "#" (3Hex | 6Hex | 8Hex)
ArrowFunction  ::= "()" "=>" RawExpressionText
Comments       ::= { LineComment | BlockComment }
Semicolons     ::= { ";" }
```

`Namespace` must already be registered and valid. Inline call namespaces such
as `synth.noise()` are rejected; put the namespace in `search`, or use
`from(namespace, call())` to override resolution for one call. Positional and
keyword arguments cannot be mixed.

`read(oN)` has two different legal roles:

1. At a chain head it becomes builtin `_read` and establishes the current input.
2. As a value for a `surface` global it resolves directly to that surface (or a
   nested call/chain can be expanded to a temporary surface).

`.read(oN)` after another chain element is illegal and produces `S001`. A chain
statement must have an explicit terminal `write(...)` or `write3d(...)`; a
separate `render(oN)` chooses presentation but does not satisfy the write
requirement. `render()` accepts only an `OUTPUT_REF` token.

Numeric arithmetic is constant-folded during parsing. Operators accept only
`Number` nodes; applying arithmetic to another expression throws `SyntaxError:
Expected number`. Division by zero is not rejected and can produce `Infinity`.
Array elements reach argument validation; non-number elements become `0` plus
an `S002` warning. Arrow-function bodies are captured as raw text and compiled
with `new Function` during validation.

Special value calls are transformed by the parser:

```ts
osc(type = oscKind.sine, min = 0, max = 1, speed = 1, offset = 0, seed = 1)
midi(channel, mode = midiMode.velocity, min = 0, max = 1, sensitivity = 1)
audio(band, min = 0, max = 1)
from(namespaceIdentifier, callExpression)
```

For oscillators, MIDI, and audio, `min` and `max` are clamped to `[0, 1]` in the
compiled automation object. `midi` requires `channel`; `audio` requires `band`.
Only `osc` rejects unknown keyword names explicitly.

Disallowed forms include: no `search`; multiple/late `search`; multiple
`render`; non-output `render`; namespaced call syntax; mixed positional/keyword
arguments; `.write()` inside an expression; missing/invalid write targets;
empty subchains; a starter in a non-head position; a non-starter at a chain
head; inline `.read`/`.read3d`; unquoted strings for most value types; and
unknown arguments.

Sources: `shaders/src/lang/lexer.js` — `lex()`;
`shaders/src/lang/parser.js` — `parseProgram()`, `parseCall()`,
`parsePrimary()`; `shaders/src/lang/validator.js` — `compileChainStatement()`,
`processChain()`.

### Validate

Use these falsifiable stages:

| Stage | Measurement | Pass threshold |
|---|---|---|
| lex | token list ends with one `EOF` | exactly one terminal `EOF`; no thrown `SyntaxError` |
| parse | `ParsedProgram.namespace.searchOrder.length` | `>= 1` |
| semantic | `diagnostics.filter(d => d.severity === "error").length` | `=== 0` |
| expansion | `graph.passes.length` and `graph.renderSurface` | passes `>= 1` and render surface non-null |
| backend compile | every referenced program in backend program map | compile completes without throw |
| execution | `pipeline.lastPassCount` after a frame | equals number of non-skipped expanded passes after repeat expansion |

Warnings (`S002`, `S007`, `S008`) do not make `compileGraph()` fail. Any semantic
error does. `runDslProgram` is a convenient combined validator on WebGL2; its
exact contract and WebGPU limitation are in the MCP section.

Sources: `shaders/src/lang/index.js` — `compile()`;
`shaders/src/runtime/compiler.js` — `compileGraph()`;
`shaders/src/runtime/pipeline.js` — `compilePrograms()`, `render()`.

### Diagnose

Declared diagnostic codes are:

| Code | Stage/severity | Implemented use |
|---|---|---|
| `L001` | lexer/error | declared only; lexer throws raw unexpected-character `SyntaxError` |
| `L002` | lexer/error | declared only; lexer throws raw unterminated-comment/string errors |
| `P001` | parser/error | declared only; parser throws raw `SyntaxError` messages |
| `P002` | parser/error | declared only; parser throws raw `SyntaxError` messages |
| `S001` | semantic/error | unknown effect/argument/enum, invalid surface, illegal inline read, invalid function, and other semantic failures |
| `S002` | semantic/warning | clamping, wrong numeric/vector/array form |
| `S003` | semantic/error | unresolved identifier or enum choice |
| `S004` | semantic/error | assigning `null` or `undefined` |
| `S005` | semantic/error | illegal starter/chain/input position |
| `S006` | semantic/error | starter chain missing terminal write |
| `S007` | semantic/warning | deprecated parameter alias |
| `S008` | semantic/warning | deprecated effect alias |
| `R001` | runtime/error | declared but not emitted by the compiler/backends shown here |

Raw lexer/parser failures are `SyntaxError` instances whose messages contain
text plus line/column for most, but not all, cases. They do not have `code`,
`severity`, or a separately typed location. `compileGraph()` only wraps semantic
diagnostics as `ERR_COMPILATION_FAILED`; raw lex/parse exceptions escape.

Expansion failures have shape `ExpansionFailure`. Current enumerated messages
are `Effect '<name>' not found` with the failing step, and `No render surface
specified and no write() found - add render(oN) or write(oN)` without a step.

Sources: `shaders/src/lang/diagnostics.js` — default export;
`shaders/src/lang/lexer.js` — thrown `SyntaxError` sites;
`shaders/src/lang/parser.js` — thrown `SyntaxError` sites;
`shaders/src/runtime/compiler.js` — `compileGraph()`;
`shaders/src/runtime/expander.js` — `expand()`.

<!-- SURFACE:EFFECT -->
## Effect definition

### Introspect

After loading, call `getEffect(name)`/`getAllEffects()` to obtain the live class
instance. `analyzeEffect` returns a JSON projection of the definition plus
combined `.glsl`/`.wgsl` sources. `checkEffectStructure` returns filesystem and
cross-language structure signals. Before loading, inspect
`shaders/effects/<namespace>/<effect>/definition.js` and the generated
`shaders/effects/manifest.json` entry.

An effect directory has this consumed layout:

```text
<namespace>/<effect>/
  definition.js             required by Noisemaker catalog
  glsl/<program>.glsl       combined WebGL2 source, or .vert + .frag
  wgsl/<program>.wgsl       WebGPU source
  help.md                    optional UI/help content
```

The manifest records each program as GLSL `"combined"` or split `{v:1,f:1}`
and WGSL `1`. `loadEffectShaders()` only loads files declared in the manifest;
a failed fetch silently leaves the shader bucket empty, so the missing-source
failure occurs later in backend compilation.

Sources: `shaders/src/runtime/registry.js` — `getEffect()`, `getAllEffects()`;
`shaders/src/renderer/canvas.js` — `loadEffectShaders()`;
`shaders/scripts/generate-shader-manifest.mjs`;
Shade MCP `src/tools/knowledge/analyze-effect.ts` — `registerAnalyzeEffect()`.

### Act

The full constructor-accepted and downstream-consumed structural grammar is
below. Inline annotations distinguish copied-only fields from fields with a
production consumer:

```ts
interface EffectDefinition {
  // Copied by new Effect(config)
  name: string
  namespace?: string
  func?: string
  description?: string
  tags?: string[]
  globals?: Record<string, GlobalSpec>
  passes: PassSpec[]
  textures?: Record<string, TextureSpec2D>
  outputTex3d?: string
  outputGeo?: string
  uniformLayout?: UniformLayout
  uniformLayouts?: Record<string, UniformLayout>
  paramAliases?: Record<string, string>
  openCategories?: string[]
  defaultProgram?: string
  hidden?: boolean
  deprecatedBy?: string
  // Copied by Effect, but no production renderer caller exists (GAP-026).
  onInit?: (this: EffectHookThis) => void
  onUpdate?: (this: EffectHookThis,
              context: { time: number, delta: number,
                         uniforms: Record<string, unknown> }) =>
              Record<string, unknown> | void
  onDestroy?: (this: EffectHookThis) => void
  // Copied and invoked by Pipeline after texture allocation.
  asyncInit?: (this: EffectHookThis, context: AsyncInitContext) => Promise<void>

  // Consumed by expander/loader when exposed by a subclass or assigned later.
  // The base Effect constructor does NOT copy these config keys.
  outputTex?: string
  outputXyz?: string
  outputVel?: string
  outputRgba?: string
  textures3d?: Record<string, TextureSpec3D>
  externalTexture?: string
  shaders?: Record<string, ProgramSources> // populated by loader in normal use

  // Observed in current definitions/manifests but not consumed by Effect,
  // expander, or Pipeline as effect-definition contracts.
  externalMesh?: string
  builtinMeshes?: unknown
}

type ProgramSources = {
  glsl?: string
  source?: string
  fragment?: string
  vertex?: string
  wgsl?: string
  vertexWGSL?: string
  vertexWgsl?: string
  fragmentEntryPoint?: string
  vertexEntryPoint?: string
  computeEntryPoint?: string
  outputFormat?: string
  topology?: string
  uniformLayout?: UniformLayout
  defines?: Record<string, boolean | number | string>
}

type UniformLayout =
  | Array<{ name: string, slot: NonNegativeInteger, components: number }>
  | Record<string, { slot: NonNegativeInteger, components: number }>

type AsyncInitContext = {
  updateTexture: (textureName: string, canvas: HTMLCanvasElement) => void
  width: number
  height: number
  params: Record<string, unknown>
  isCancelled: () => boolean
}

type EffectHookThis = InstanceType<typeof Effect> & {
  state: Record<string, unknown>
  uniforms: Record<string, unknown>
}
```

Use `export default new Effect({...})` for data-only definitions. Use a subclass
when you must expose consumed fields the base constructor does not copy or
override lifecycle methods. Do not assume an arbitrary config property is
copied: the constructor uses an explicit allowlist.

When an `Effect` wrapper method invokes a config hook, it uses `.call(this)`
with the live instance, not the definition object. `this.state` and
`this.uniforms` are initialized to empty objects before config fields are
copied; subclasses can add more instance members. The production pipeline
invokes `asyncInit()` after texture allocation. No production code invokes
`onInit()`, `onUpdate()`, or `onDestroy()`; only the test harness does. Treat
those three as copied callbacks, not working production lifecycle authoring
points. This is GAP-026.

`func` is the DSL call name. The loader registers the same instance under
`func`, `namespace.func`, `namespace/effectDirectory`, and
`namespace.effectDirectory`. `globals` insertion order is positional DSL
argument order; append new optional arguments after existing positional
arguments to preserve old calls.

Sources: `shaders/src/runtime/effect.js` — `Effect.constructor`, lifecycle
methods; `shaders/src/renderer/canvas.js` — `registerEffectWithRuntime()`;
`shaders/src/runtime/expander.js` — `expand()`.

### Validate

`validateEffectDefinition(def)` returns `string[]`. Its only pass predicate is
`errors.length === 0`. It checks:

- `name` is a non-empty string.
- `passes` is a non-empty array.
- every pass has a string `program`.
- present pass `inputs` and `outputs` are objects.
- present `globals` is an object.
- every global has a truthy `type`.

That validator does not prove the complete grammar. A development gate must
also require:

1. `checkEffectStructure.status === "ok"`.
2. `compileEffect` returns `status === "ok"` for WebGL2.
3. `compileEffect` returns `status === "ok"` for WebGPU when parity is required.
4. `renderEffectFrame.status === "ok"` and the output-specific predicates in the
   Rendered output section pass.
5. `testPixelParity.status === "ok"` for parity-authorable effects.

`checkEffectStructure.status === "warning"` when any implemented structural
issue is present. The precise issue arrays are in its MCP contract.

Sources: `shaders/src/runtime/effect-validator.js` —
`validateEffectDefinition()`; Shade MCP `src/tools/analysis/structure.ts` —
`checkEffectStructure()`.

### Diagnose

The complete definition-validator strings are:

```text
Effect definition is null or undefined
Missing or invalid "name" property
Missing or empty "passes" array
Pass <index>: Missing "program" string
Pass <index>: "inputs" must be an object
Pass <index>: "outputs" must be an object
"globals" must be an object
Global '<key>': Missing "type"
```

Asset/load failures include:

```text
No default export in <effectId>/definition.js
No default export in bundle <effectId>
Invalid effect ID: <effectId>
Could not load shader manifest - lazy loading requires manifest.json
```

Backend compilation then reports missing shader source, shader compilation,
linking, or program-spec failures enumerated under Cross-backend parity.

Unknown fields, wrong tag values, wrong global defaults/ranges, wrong pass field
values, texture shape, shader/declaration binding agreement, and lifecycle
signatures are not checked by `validateEffectDefinition()`. See GAP-003.

Sources: `shaders/src/runtime/effect-validator.js` —
`validateEffectDefinition()`; `shaders/src/renderer/canvas.js` — load/import
failure sites; backend `compileProgram()` methods.

<!-- SURFACE:GLOBALS -->
## Parameters and globals

### Introspect

The authoritative author-facing map is `effect.globals`. After registration,
`ops[namespace.func].args` is the DSL projection, in positional order. After
expansion, `graph.passes[].uniforms` contains resolved defaults, DSL arguments,
upstream inherited uniforms, pass-level mappings, and internal globals.
`graph.passes[].uniformSpecs` contains numeric automation ranges keyed by shader
uniform name.

Current definition census contains these `type` strings:

```text
boolean color float geometry int mat3 member palette string surface
vec2 vec3 vec4 volume
```

The runtime has explicit argument branches for `surface`, `color`, `vec3`,
`vec4`, `volume`, `geometry`, and `string`; other types use the numeric/general
branch. During DSL registration, `vec4` is projected as DSL type `color`.

Current observed global keys are:

```text
choices colorModeUniform default define enum max min randChance randChoices
randMax randMin step type ui uniform zero
```

`type`, `default`, `uniform`, `min`, `max`, `choices`, `enum`, `define`, and
`colorModeUniform` affect compilation/expansion. `step`, `ui`, and randomization
metadata are UI/program-generation policy, not shader binding validation.

Sources: `shaders/effects/**/definition.js` — `globals` census;
`shaders/src/renderer/canvas.js` — `registerEffectWithRuntime()`;
`shaders/src/lang/validator.js` — argument type branches;
`shaders/src/runtime/expander.js` — global/uniform mapping.

### Act

```ts
interface GlobalSpec {
  type: "float" | "int" | "boolean" | "member" | "surface" | "volume" |
        "geometry" | "color" | "vec2" | "vec3" | "vec4" | "mat3" |
        "palette" | "string"
  default?: unknown
  uniform?: string
  min?: number
  max?: number
  step?: number
  enum?: string                 // dotted enum root, e.g. "channel"
  choices?: Record<string, number | string | null> // null entries are group labels
  define?: string               // compile-time GLSL macro / WGSL const name
  colorModeUniform?: string     // 0 for surface "none", otherwise 1
  zero?: unknown                // observed program-generation metadata
  randMin?: number              // observed randomization metadata
  randMax?: number
  randChance?: number
  randChoices?: unknown[]
  ui?: {
    label?: string
    control?: "slider" | "checkbox" | "dropdown" | "color" | "button" |
              "vec3" | "vector3" | false
    category?: string
    hidden?: boolean
    enabledBy?: EnableCondition
    hint?: string
    multiline?: boolean
    buttonLabel?: string
  }
}

type EnableCondition =
  | string // legacy parameter-name truthiness check
  | {
      or?: EnableCondition[]
      and?: EnableCondition[]
      not?: EnableCondition
      param?: string
      eq?: unknown
      neq?: unknown
      gt?: number
      gte?: number
      lt?: number
      lte?: number
      in?: unknown[]
      notIn?: unknown[]
    }
```

Binding pipeline:

1. The object key is the DSL parameter name and positional slot.
2. `registerEffectWithRuntime()` maps `vec4` to DSL `color`, copies
   `default/min/max/uniform/choices`, and sets `enum`/`enumPath`.
3. If `choices` exists without `enum`, it generates enum root
   `<namespace>.<func>.<parameter>`.
4. Choice keys ending in `:` are group labels and are not registered as enum
   identifiers. Other keys are registered as written and, when different, under
   a sanitized identifier.
5. The validator stores the resolved value under the DSL parameter name in
   `step.args`.
6. The expander binds it to `spec.uniform` when present, otherwise the DSL name.
7. `PassSpec.uniforms` can map a shader uniform name to a global parameter name.
8. `define` removes the value from runtime-uniform intent and creates a
   program-cache suffix plus a GLSL `#define` or WGSL module `const`.

Formal value contracts:

| Type | DSL form and resolution |
|---|---|
| numeric/general | number, boolean→`0/1`, enum member, matching bare enum/choice identifier, state identifier, automation, or default; clamp to present `min/max` |
| `member` | normally enum member/bare enum identifier; stored as numeric value after enum resolution |
| `surface` | `oN`, `sN`, `xyzN`, `velN`, `rgbaN`, `meshN`, `none`, `read(surface)`, or nested call/chain producing a temp; quoted string rejected |
| `volume` | `vol0`–`vol7`, `none`, or single-argument `read3d(volN)` |
| `geometry` | `geo0`–`geo7`, `none`, or single-argument `read3d(geoN)` |
| `color` | hex color; invalid form falls back to default with diagnostic |
| `vec3` | `vec3(n,n,n)`, hex color RGB, numeric array literal, or default |
| `vec4` | `vec4(n,n,n,n)`, hex RGBA, numeric array literal, or default; normal loader projects it to DSL `color` |
| `string` | quoted string only for `text.text`, `text.font`, `text.justify`; matching choice identifier also accepted |
| `palette` | numeric/general argument; expander additionally writes dependent palette uniforms already present on the pass |

`defaultFrom` exists in validator operator specs but is not copied by
`registerEffectWithRuntime()` from `GlobalSpec`; effect definitions cannot use it
through the normal loader without separate operator registration.

Sources: `shaders/src/renderer/canvas.js` —
`registerEffectWithRuntime()`; `shaders/src/lang/validator.js` — argument
resolution; `shaders/src/runtime/expander.js` — compile-time defines and uniform
mapping; `shaders/src/lang/enums.js` — `mergeIntoEnums()`;
`demo/shaders/lib/demo-ui.js` — `_evaluateEnableCondition()`.

### Validate

Numeric input pass/fail is measurable:

- Resolved numeric range is `[min,max]` when either bound exists.
- Input below `min` or above `max` is clamped and emits `S002`; compilation still
  succeeds because `S002` is a warning.
- A strict authoring gate should require zero `S002` diagnostics rather than
  treating clamping as success.
- An `int` test value in `testUniformResponsiveness` is rounded; normal DSL
  numeric validation does not round `int` arguments.

Runtime responsiveness uses two rendered mean-RGB vectors. Define:

```text
baseline_luma = (baseline_r + baseline_g + baseline_b) / 3
test_luma     = (test_r + test_g + test_b) / 3
luma_delta    = abs(test_luma - baseline_luma)
max_channel_delta = max(abs(test_r-baseline_r),
                        abs(test_g-baseline_g),
                        abs(test_b-baseline_b))
```

A tested uniform passes when `luma_delta > 0.002` OR
`max_channel_delta > 0.002`. Only globals with `uniform`, numeric `min` and
`max`, unequal bounds, and a type other than `boolean`/`button` are tested.
The test value is `min + 0.75*(max-min)` when default equals min, otherwise
`min + 0.25*(max-min)`; `int` is rounded.

Important: the tool's overall status is `ok` when **any** tested uniform passes,
even if other entries are `name:fail`. A strict effect gate must require every
`tested_uniforms` entry to end in `:pass`. See GAP-010.

Sources: `shaders/src/lang/validator.js` — `clamp()` and numeric argument
branch; Shade MCP `src/tools/browser/uniforms.ts` —
`testUniformResponsiveness()`.

### Diagnose

Parameter-related semantic failures use `Diagnostic`:

- `S001`: quoted string for a non-string type, invalid surface/volume/geometry,
  unknown argument, unresolved enum, illegal string parameter, invalid function.
- `S002`: out-of-range value with `(got <v>, clamped to <c>)`, wrong value form,
  or nonnumeric array element.
- `S003`: undefined variable, enum identifier, or inline choice.
- `S007`: a deprecated parameter alias was rewritten.

`testUniformResponsiveness` returns:

```ts
type UniformResponsiveness =
  | { status: "ok" | "skipped" | "error",
      tested_uniforms: Array<`${string}:pass` | `${string}:fail` |
                             `${string}:error`>,
      details: string,
      console_errors?: string[] }
  | { status: "error", error: string }
```

Enumerated in-page failures are `No effect loaded` and `Failed to capture
baseline`; outer browser/session failures use `{status:"error",error:string}`.
The output does not report the selected test value, baseline means, test means,
or deltas, so a failing global cannot be quantitatively diagnosed from this
tool alone. See GAP-011.

Sources: `shaders/src/lang/validator.js` — argument diagnostics;
Shade MCP `src/tools/browser/uniforms.ts` — output paths.

<!-- SURFACE:GRAPH -->
## Passes and render graph

### Introspect

`compileGraph(source)` returns the expanded graph:

```ts
type CompiledGraph = {
  id: string                         // signed 32-bit source hash in base 36
  source: string
  passes: ExpandedPass[]             // execution order; no hidden sort
  programs: Record<string, ProgramSources>
  allocations: Map<string, `phys_${number}`>
  textures: Map<string, RuntimeTextureSpec>
  renderSurface: string | null
  compiledAt: number                 // Date.now(), milliseconds since epoch
}

type ExpandedPass = {
  id: string
  program: string
  type?: "render"                    // present on generated write/final blits
  entryPoint?: string
  drawMode?: "points" | "billboards" | "triangles"
  drawBuffers?: number
  count?: number | "auto" | "screen" | "input"
  countUniform?: string
  repeat?: number | string
  blend?: boolean | [string | number, string | number]
  workgroups?: [number, number, number]
  storageBuffers?: Record<string, unknown>
  storageTextures?: Record<string, string>
  inputs: Record<string, string>      // shader sampler/binding -> virtual texture
  outputs: Record<string, string>     // fragment attachment -> virtual texture
  uniforms: Record<string, unknown>   // shader uniform -> resolved value
  uniformSpecs?: Record<string, { min: number, max: number }>
  scopedParams?: Record<string, string>
  inheritsVolumeSize?: boolean
  effectKey?: string
  effectFunc?: string
  effectNamespace?: string | null
  nodeId?: string
  stepIndex?: number
}
```

To make `Map` values machine-readable:

```js
const graphView = {
  ...graph,
  allocations: Object.fromEntries(graph.allocations),
  textures: Object.fromEntries(graph.textures),
}
```

Current connections are exactly `passes[i].inputs` and `passes[i].outputs`.
For every input entry, the key is the shader sampler/binding name and the value
is the resolved virtual texture ID. For every output entry, the key is the
shader output attachment name and the value is the resolved target ID.

Passes are appended in DSL plan order, effect-step order, and definition pass
array order. Builtin read/write/blit passes are inserted at the point the
expander encounters them. There is no topological sort, dependency object,
barrier plan, cycle detector, or `ERR_CYCLE` in current code.

Sources: `shaders/src/runtime/compiler.js` — `compileGraph()`;
`shaders/src/runtime/expander.js` — `expand()`;
`shaders/src/runtime/resources.js` — `analyzeLiveness()`,
`allocateResources()`.

### Act

The definition-level authoring grammar is:

```ts
interface PassSpec {
  program: string
  inputs?: Record<string, TextureReference>
  outputs?: Record<string, TextureReference>
  uniforms?: Record<string, string> // shader name -> GlobalSpec key/name
  entryPoint?: string
  drawMode?: "points" | "billboards" | "triangles"
  drawBuffers?: number
  count?: number | "auto" | "screen" | "input"
  countUniform?: string
  repeat?: number | string
  blend?: boolean | [BlendFactor, BlendFactor]
  workgroups?: [number, number, number]
  storageBuffers?: Record<string, unknown>
  storageTextures?: Record<string, string>

  // Present in current definitions, but expand() does not copy these fields.
  // They are observed, not authorable runtime controls through definition.js.
  name?: string
  conditions?: PassConditions
  viewport?: { x: number, y: number, w: number, h: number }

  // Pipeline/backends understand these on a manually constructed graph pass,
  // but expand() does not copy them from PassSpec.
  clear?: boolean
  samplerTypes?: Record<string, "default" | "nearest" | "repeat">
  type?: "render" | "compute"
}

type TextureReference = string
type BlendFactor =
  | "ZERO" | "ONE" | "SRC_COLOR" | "ONE_MINUS_SRC_COLOR"
  | "DST_COLOR" | "ONE_MINUS_DST_COLOR" | "SRC_ALPHA"
  | "ONE_MINUS_SRC_ALPHA" | "DST_ALPHA" | "ONE_MINUS_DST_ALPHA"
  | "CONSTANT_COLOR" | "ONE_MINUS_CONSTANT_COLOR" | "CONSTANT_ALPHA"
  | "ONE_MINUS_CONSTANT_ALPHA" | "SRC_ALPHA_SATURATE"
  | "zero" | "one" | "src" | "one-minus-src" | "dst"
  | "one-minus-dst" | "src-alpha" | "one-minus-src-alpha"
  | "dst-alpha" | "one-minus-dst-alpha"
  | number

type PassConditions = {
  skipIf?: Array<{ uniform: string, equals: unknown }>
  runIf?: Array<{ uniform: string, equals: unknown }>
}
```

Input resolution rules, in priority order for each `inputs` entry:

1. `inputTex` and `o<digits>` use the current 2D chain input or the literal
   reference when no input exists.
2. `inputTex3d`, `inputGeo`, `inputXyz`, `inputVel`, and `inputRgba` use the
   corresponding upstream channel or remain literal.
3. `noise` becomes `global_noise`; `midiNoteGrid` remains `midiNoteGrid`.
4. `feedback`/`selfTex` maps to the chain write surface, or current input.
5. A matching surface global in `step.args` resolves a temp, current pipeline
   input, named global surface, or `none`.
6. A matching `GlobalSpec.default` resolves `none`, input, global surface,
   chain-scoped `global_...`, or literal name.
7. An explicit `global_...` value is chain/particle scoped.
8. `outputTex` refers to this effect node's primary output.
9. Anything else is a node-local texture and becomes `<nodeId>_<name>`.

Output resolution rules:

- `outputTex` becomes the node primary output. The last pass of the last step
  can write directly to the chain's global surface.
- `outputTex3d`, `outputXyz`, `outputVel`, and `outputRgba` create typed node
  outputs.
- `inputTex3d`, `inputGeo`, `inputXyz`, `inputVel`, `inputRgba` write in place to
  their upstream channel when one exists.
- `global_...` is chain/particle scoped; `feedback_...` remains literal;
  otherwise the target is node-local.

`uniforms: {shaderName: globalName}` reads the already resolved pipeline value,
then the referenced global's default. It does not declare a new global.

`repeat` is resolved at execution. A number becomes
`max(1,floor(repeat))`. A string looks up `globalUniforms[name]`, then
`pass.uniforms[name]`; numeric values use `max(1,floor(value))`, otherwise one.
Repeated writes advance frame-local ping-pong bindings after every iteration.

`drawBuffers > 1` or more than one output activates MRT. `blend: true` is
additive one/one. A two-item blend array selects explicit factors. Unknown
WebGL factors fall back to `ONE`; WebGPU lowercases/replaces underscores and
lets device validation reject invalid factors.

`type` does not select a WebGPU compute program. WebGPU detects a compute-only
program from `@compute` without `@fragment`; WebGL detects compute-style work
from `storageTextures` or an `outputBuffer` output and applies a fragment-pass
fallback.

Sources: `shaders/src/runtime/expander.js` — pass construction and input/output
mapping; `shaders/src/runtime/pipeline.js` — `render()`,
`resolveRepeatCount()`; backend `executePass()` and blend resolvers.

### Validate

A source-grounded graph gate can assert:

```text
semantic_error_count === 0
expansion_error_count === 0
passes.length >= 1
renderSurface !== null
every pass.program resolves in graph.programs
every pass output is a nonempty string
every pass input is a nonempty string or intentionally omitted for null arg
every non-global produced texture has an allocations entry
```

`analyzeLiveness(passes)` returns `{start,end}` indices for every non-`global_`
texture touched as an input or output. Both indices are in
`[0, passes.length-1]` and `start <= end`. `allocateResources()` assigns
`phys_0`, `phys_1`, ... to non-global outputs and reuses a slot only when its
previous texture's `end < currentPassIndex`.

The allocation map is an analysis result only: no pipeline/backend consumer
reads `graph.allocations`. Therefore it validates the analyzer, not actual GPU
pooling. See GAP-006.

There is no dependency/cycle validator. A pass that reads a texture before a
producer can compile and may sample a default, stale, or missing texture. A
formal authoring gate must perform its own source-order check:

```text
For each non-global, non-external input at pass i:
  some pass j < i must produce it, OR it must be intentionally frame-persistent.
```

That predicate is recommended policy, not an engine-emitted result.

Sources: `shaders/src/runtime/resources.js` — liveness/allocation;
repository search for `graph.allocations` (creation only); backend texture
binding fallbacks.

### Diagnose

Expansion uses `ExpansionFailure`. Graph/backend failures include:

```ts
{ code: "ERR_PROGRAM_SPEC_MISSING", program: string, pass: string }
{ code: "ERR_PROGRAM_NOT_FOUND", program: string, pass: string }
{ code: "ERR_TEXTURE_NOT_FOUND", texture: string, pass: string } // WebGPU
{ code: "ERR_NO_MRT_OUTPUTS", pass: string }                     // WebGPU
{ code: "ERR_COMPUTE_DISPATCH_UNRESOLVED", pass: string,
  detail: "Compute dispatch dimensions could not be inferred" }
```

WebGL2 missing FBOs and MRT textures are console warnings, not throws. WebGL
errors discovered after a draw are logged with pass/effect/program/output/input
context and drained, not converted into a structured failure. WebGPU missing MRT
members are warnings until all outputs are absent; device validation arrives as
an `uncapturederror` console message. These non-structured paths are GAP-007.

Definition fields `name`, `conditions`, `viewport`, `clear`, `samplerTypes`, and
`type` are not copied by `expand()` even though downstream code understands
some of them. `conditions` in checked-in definitions therefore does not reach
`Pipeline.shouldSkipPass()`. This is GAP-005.

Sources: `shaders/src/runtime/pipeline.js` — `compilePrograms()`, `render()`,
`shouldSkipPass()`; backend `executePass()` implementations;
`shaders/src/runtime/expander.js` — constructed pass keys.

<!-- SURFACE:TEXTURES -->
## Textures and surface resources

### Introspect

Definition texture specs are available at `effect.textures` (2D) and, for
class-based effects, `effect.textures3d`. Expanded/scoped specs are returned in
`graph.textures: Map<string,RuntimeTextureSpec>`. Allocated backend records are
in `pipeline.backend.textures`; double-buffer records are in
`pipeline.surfaces`.

```ts
type RuntimeTextureSpec = {
  width: DimensionSpec
  height: DimensionSpec
  depth?: DimensionSpec
  format: string
  is3D?: boolean
  usage: Array<"render" | "sample" | "storage" | "copySrc" | "copyDst">
}

type SurfaceRecord2D = {
  read: string
  write: string
  currentFrame: number
}

type MeshSurfaceRecord = {
  positions: string
  normals: string
  uvs: string
  width: number
  height: number
}
```

Node-local texture names are prefixed with `node_<step>_`. Non-particle names
beginning `global_` are chain-scoped. Particle state names are scoped to the
creating particle node. Normal global surfaces have `_read`/`_write` backend
textures.

Sources: `shaders/src/runtime/expander.js` — texture-spec collection/scoping;
`shaders/src/runtime/compiler.js` — `extractTextureSpecs()`;
`shaders/src/runtime/pipeline.js` — `createSurfaces()`, `recreateTextures()`.

### Act

```ts
type DimensionSpec =
  | number
  | "screen"
  | "auto"
  | `${number}%`
  | { param: string, paramDefault?: number, multiply?: number,
      power?: number, default?: number }
  | { screenDivide: string, default?: number }
  | { scale: number, clamp?: { min?: number, max?: number } }

type TextureFormat2D =
  | "rgba8" | "rgba16f" | "rgba32f"
  | "r8" | "r16f" | "r32f"
  | "rgba8unorm" | "rgba16float" | "rgba32float"
  | "r8unorm" | "r16float" | "r32float"
  | "rg8" | "rg16f" | "rg32f"
  | "bgra8unorm"
  | string

interface TextureSpec2D {
  width?: DimensionSpec
  height?: DimensionSpec
  format?: TextureFormat2D
}

interface TextureSpec3D extends TextureSpec2D {
  depth?: DimensionSpec
}

type TextureSpec = TextureSpec2D | TextureSpec3D
```

Omitted/falsy definition width or height becomes `screen`; omitted format
becomes `rgba16f`. Graph-generated pass outputs also use screen size,
`rgba16f`, and usage `render/sample/copySrc/copyDst`. A 3D spec gets
`is3D:true`; its depth default is `effectSpec.depth || effectSpec.width || 64`
and usage is `storage/sample/copySrc/copyDst`.

Dimension resolution:

| Spec | Resolved pixels |
|---|---|
| number | `max(1,floor(spec))` |
| `screen` / `auto` | the corresponding pipeline screen dimension |
| percentage string | `max(1,floor(screenSize * parseFloat(spec)/100))` |
| `{param}` | uniform value or `paramDefault` or 64; then multiply/power; transformed missing-param can use final `default`; floor and clamp to at least 1 |
| `{screenDivide}` | `max(1,round(screenSize/(uniform or default or 1)))` |
| `{scale}` | floor of screenSize×scale, optional min/max clamp, then at least 1 |
| any other value/string | screen size |

`"input"` appears in existing definitions but has no special resolver branch;
it falls through to screen size. It does not query the input texture.

WebGL2 2D textures always use nearest minification/magnification and clamp-to-
edge. Its recognized format map is exactly `rgba8`, `rgba16f`, `rgba32f`, `r8`,
`r16f`, `r32f`; any other string, including WebGPU spelling
`rgba8unorm`, falls back to RGBA8. WebGL2 3D textures use linear filtering
and cannot be render targets. Although `createTexture3D()` can honor
`spec.filter === "nearest"` on a manually constructed runtime spec,
`extractTextureSpecs()` does not copy that key. A definition-level `filter` is discarded and is not authorable through `definition.js`.

WebGPU maps the six WebGL spellings plus `rg8/rg16f/rg32f`, accepts listed WebGPU
spellings, and otherwise returns the provided string to WebGPU validation. The
normal surface sampler defaults to nearest for parity with WebGL2; external
uploads use the default linear sampler.

There is no 2D mip field, mip-level allocation, or mip generation path. There
is no authorable `persistent`, `transient`, or 3D filter field. Do not emit
them. See GAP-004.

Sources: `shaders/src/runtime/pipeline.js` — `resolveDimension()`;
`shaders/src/runtime/backends/webgl2.js` — `createTexture()`,
`createTexture3D()`, `resolveFormat()`;
`shaders/src/runtime/backends/webgpu.js` — texture creation, samplers,
`resolveFormat()`.

### Validate

Measurable resource predicates after `pipeline.resize(width,height)`:

```text
every resolved non-screen dimension >= 1
every graph texture has backend record with matching width and height
every 3D graph texture has matching depth and is3D === true
every global 2D surface has distinct read and write texture IDs
every pass output intended for WebGL rendering has an FBO
every format is recognized by the target backend before compilation/execution
```

WebGL FBO completeness is checked during creation but only logged. WebGPU device
creation validates size/format/usage asynchronously through normal exceptions
or `uncapturederror`. There is no public aggregate resource-validation report.

Frame lifetime is behavior, not an authoring flag:

- Backend texture objects survive frames until resize, recompilation/recreation,
  or `dispose()`.
- WebGL passes do not clear color targets before a normal draw.
- WebGPU normal render attachments use `loadOp:"load"` unless `pass.clear`, but
  `clear` is not copied from effect definitions.
- Global surfaces are double-buffered. Display surfaces swap at frame end.
  Names classified as state (`xyz`, `vel`, `rgba`, `trail`, suffix variants,
  names containing `state`, scoped particle state) preserve final frame-local
  bindings.
- A node-local texture can therefore retain data across frames, but the engine
  exposes no declaration, preservation-on-resize guarantee, or queryable
  persistent/transient class.

Sources: backend texture/FBO creation; `shaders/src/runtime/pipeline.js` —
`swapBuffers()`, `resize()`, `dispose()`.

### Diagnose

Resource failures include:

```text
WebGL Error: Texture <textureId> not found                 (raw Error in readPixels)
WebGPU: {code:"ERR_TEXTURE_NOT_FOUND", pass, texture}
WebGL console: FBO incomplete for texture <id>: <status>
WebGL console: MRT FBO incomplete for <id>: <status>
WebGL console: [executePass] FBO not found for <id> in pass <pass>
WebGPU console: Storage texture <id> not found
WebGPU uncaptured error: <device validation message>
```

WebGL unknown formats silently become RGBA8; WebGPU unknown formats reach device
validation. Unknown dimension forms silently become screen size. Neither path
returns a structured authoring diagnostic. There is no mip/persistence
rejection because those keys are not validated and are ignored. See GAP-004 and
GAP-007.

Sources: backend texture and pass execution methods;
`shaders/src/runtime/pipeline.js` — `resolveDimension()`.

<!-- SURFACE:MUTATION -->
## Compatibility and mutation

### Introspect

Compile the DSL, then call `listSteps(compiled)`:

```ts
type StepInfo = {
  stepIndex: number
  planIndex: number
  chainIndex: number
  effectName: string
  isStarter: boolean
  isStarterPosition: boolean
  canReplaceWithStarter: boolean
  canReplaceWithNonStarter: boolean
  args: Record<string, unknown>
}
```

`stepIndex` is the compiled step's `temp`. Branch objects do not have `chain`
and are skipped by these mutation helpers. `listSteps()` does not filter for
effect nodes: it returns every member of `plan.chain`, including the terminal
`write` builtin. A two-effect chain ending in `.write(o0)` therefore returns
three entries. `getCompatibleReplacements()` enumerates the currently
registered `ops` and partitions them by starter state.

Sources: `shaders/src/lang/transform.js` — `listSteps()`,
`getCompatibleReplacements()`; `shaders/src/lang/ops.js` — `ops`;
`shaders/tests/test_transform.js` — stale effect-only length assertions
(GAP-022).

### Act

```ts
type ReplacementQuery =
  | { success: true, compatible: string[], incompatible: string[] }
  | { success: false, error: string }

type ReplacementResult =
  | { success: true, program: CompiledProgram }
  | { success: false, error: string }

getCompatibleReplacements(
  compiled: CompiledProgram,
  stepIndex: number,
  options?: { searchOrder?: string[] }
): ReplacementQuery

replaceEffect(
  compiled: CompiledProgram,
  stepIndex: number,
  newEffectName: string,
  newArgs?: Record<string, unknown>,
  options?: { searchOrder?: string[] }
): ReplacementResult
```

The checkable current definition of compatible is exactly:

```text
target effect exists in ops
AND
(target chainIndex === 0) === isStarterOp(target effect)
```

No other shape participates. On success, `replaceEffect` deep-clones the
compiled program, starts with every target argument default, overlays
`newArgs`, rounds non-integer numeric `newArgs` to three decimal places, sets
the resolved operator name, resets namespace metadata, and adds the namespace
to `searchNamespaces` if needed.

Sources: `shaders/src/lang/transform.js` — `replaceEffect()`.

### Validate

Mutation succeeds only when `result.success === true`. That proves effect
existence and starter-position compatibility. It does **not** prove the mutated
program compiles or renders. A complete falsifiable gate is:

```text
replacement.success === true
AND unparse(replacement.program) parses
AND semantic_error_count === 0
AND expansion_error_count === 0
AND both required backend compilers succeed
AND rendered-output thresholds pass
AND parity status is ok when required
```

`getCompatibleReplacements().compatible` is therefore a candidate list, not a
swappability guarantee. See GAP-008.

Sources: `shaders/tests/test_transform.js`; compiler, render, and parity
contracts in this document.

### Diagnose

Enumerated mutation rejection strings are:

```text
Invalid compiled program: missing plans
Step with index <stepIndex> not found
Effect '<newEffectName>' not found
Cannot replace starter effect '<old>' with non-starter effect '<new>'. The first effect in a chain must be a starting effect.
Cannot replace non-starter effect '<old>' with starter effect '<new>'. Starting effects can only appear at the beginning of a chain.
```

After a successful replacement, all parse, semantic, expansion, backend, output,
and parity failures use their respective surfaces' diagnostic channels.
Parameter names/types/ranges, required textures, pass shape, shader availability,
and backend support are absent from compatibility preflight. This missing
queryable shape contract is GAP-008.

The same unfiltered-chain behavior exposes terminal `write` as a replaceable
nonstarter step. Callers must exclude builtin names themselves because
`StepInfo` has no node-kind discriminator. This is GAP-022.

Sources: `shaders/src/lang/transform.js` — all `success:false` paths.

<!-- SURFACE:OUTPUT -->
## Rendered output

### Introspect

`renderEffectFrame` samples the WebGL default framebuffer after warmup. The
decoded success payload is:

```ts
type ImageMetrics = {
  mean_rgb: Vec3                 // each component in [0,1]
  mean_alpha: number             // [0,1]
  std_rgb: Vec3                  // each population stddev in [0,0.5]
  luma_variance: number          // population variance in [0,0.25]
  unique_sampled_colors: number  // integer in [1,sample_count]
  is_all_zero: boolean
  is_all_transparent: boolean
  is_essentially_blank: boolean
  is_monochrome: boolean
}

type RenderSuccess = {
  effect_id: string
  status: "ok"
  backend: string
  frame: { image_uri: string | null, width: number, height: number }
  metrics: ImageMetrics
  console_errors?: string[]
}

type RenderFailure = {
  effect_id?: string
  status: "error"
  backend?: "unknown" | string
  error: string
  console_errors?: string[]
}
```

The sample stride is
`max(1,floor(width*height/1000))`; every `stride`th pixel is sampled. Therefore
sample count is `ceil(width*height/stride)` and can exceed 1000 (for the worked
90×90 capture it is 1013). Colors are exact 8-bit RGB triplets; alpha is not
part of color cardinality.

Sources: Shade MCP `src/tools/browser/render.ts` —
`renderEffectFrame()`; `src/harness/types.ts` — `ImageMetrics`, `RenderResult`.

### Act

Call the MCP verb documented in the tool section, or render through
`CanvasRenderer`/`Pipeline` and read `pipeline.getOutput()` with backend
readback. For deterministic MCP captures, omit `time`; the current tool pauses
before waiting for warmup frames when `time` is present and can deadlock. See
GAP-014.

Uniform override keys are shader uniform names as consumed by
`pipeline.setUniform`, not necessarily DSL global names. `resolution` changes
the browser viewport; it does not guarantee the internal canvas dimensions,
as shown by the worked call requesting 256×256 and capturing 90×90.

Sources: Shade MCP `src/tools/browser/render.ts` — request handling;
Noisemaker `shaders/src/runtime/pipeline.js` — `setUniform()`, `getOutput()`.

### Validate

The implemented predicates are exactly:

```text
is_all_zero         = mean_r === 0 && mean_g === 0 && mean_b === 0
is_all_transparent  = mean_alpha < 0.01
is_essentially_blank= luma_variance < 0.0001
is_monochrome       = unique_sampled_colors <= 1
```

For an ordinary non-exempt effect, the repository harness requires:

```text
status === "ok"
is_all_transparent === false
is_essentially_blank === false
is_monochrome === false
console error count === 0
```

`is_essentially_blank` means low spatial luma variance, not black. A constant
white image satisfies it. The harness exemptions are:

- monochrome: `filter/a`, `filter/shape`, `filter/solid`, `synth/solid`.
- low-luma-variance: `synth/solid`.
- transparency: none.

There is no implemented no-animation metric or pass threshold. There is no
implemented low-variety threshold between one and many colors. Use of a made-up
`unique_sampled_colors > N` gate would not be source-grounded. These are
GAP-009.

The separate filter modification tool uses `temporalDiff > 0.01` OR
`uniqueColors > 5`, but that predicate says "modifies input" and cannot
serve as a general animation/variety validator.

The repository sustained benchmark gate uses median frame time after 20 warmup
frames and 100 measured frames and fails when `fps < 30`; equivalently it passes
when `fps >= 30`. The MCP benchmark instead passes when
`fps >= target_fps`, where the default target is 60.

Sources: Shade MCP `src/tools/browser/render.ts`, `passthrough.ts`,
`benchmark.ts`; `shaders/tests/test-harness.js` — metric gates, exemptions, and
sustained benchmark.

### Diagnose

In-page render failures are:

```text
No renderer
Failed to read pixels
```

Outer browser/session failures use `{effect_id,status:"error",error:string}`.
Captured console messages can appear as `console_errors`, but backend console
diagnostics are strings rather than typed error objects.

The current upstream `renderEffectFrame` reads only `pipeline.backend.gl` and
therefore returns `Failed to read pixels` on WebGPU. Noisemaker's vendored test
harness supplies its own WebGPU `backend.readPixels()` wrapper; that wrapper is
not the MCP verb contract. See GAP-012.

The shared/vendored image-metric helper uses a different "essentially blank"
definition (dark mean plus quantized color cardinality) than the current MCP
verb's luma-variance predicate. Consumers must use the verb implementation for
the fields documented here. This implementation drift is GAP-015.

Sources: Shade MCP `src/tools/browser/render.ts`; vendored
`vendor/shade-mcp/harness/pixel-reader.js`; `shaders/tests/test-harness.js` —
WebGPU render wrapper.

<!-- SURFACE:PARITY -->
## Cross-backend parity

### Introspect

Before rendering, inspect the manifest and `checkEffectStructure`:

- every referenced program must have a GLSL `.glsl` or `.vert`/`.frag` entry;
- every referenced program must have a same-stem WGSL `.wgsl` entry;
- `structuralParityIssues` must be empty;
- `compareShaders` exposes line counts, detected functions, and detected uniform
  declarations, but does not establish semantic equivalence;
- `checkAlgEquiv` is an optional AI judgment, not a numeric parity signal.

Backend authorability from current source:

| Feature | WebGL2 authoring | WebGPU authoring | Predictable parity status |
|---|---|---|---|
| fullscreen fragment | GLSL ES 3 combined fragment or split vertex/fragment | WGSL fragment, optional WGSL vertex | authorable when both sources/bindings implement same math |
| MRT | multiple fragment outputs; device draw-buffer limit | multiple `@location` outputs/attachments | authorable within both devices' attachment limits |
| points/billboards/triangles | draw modes supported | draw modes supported | authorable; coordinate/winding conventions still require pixel test |
| compile-time define | GLSL `#define` | typed WGSL module `const` | authorable for boolean/numeric compatible uses |
| native compute | absent | compute-only WGSL selected from `@compute` | impossible as native parity |
| compute-style fallback | hand-authored GLSL fragment plus `storageTextures`/output mapping | native compute/storage | only authorable when the GLSL fragment explicitly reproduces the algorithm; no translation exists |
| storage buffers | no equivalent binding path | parsed/bound by WebGPU | generally impossible parity without a different GLSL algorithm |
| 3D sampling | supported | supported | authorable |
| rendering directly to 3D texture | explicitly unsupported | storage/render workflows available | impossible direct parity; use a 2D atlas/common algorithm |

There is no single capability-preflight API that consumes a definition and
returns this determination. `pipeline.getCapabilities()` reports device limits,
not effect authorability. See GAP-016.

Sources: `shaders/src/renderer/canvas.js` — `loadEffectShaders()`;
backend `compileProgram()`, `executePass()`, `createTexture3D()`;
Shade MCP analysis tools.

### Act

Author same-stem shader programs and keep binding names/types aligned with the
expanded pass. WebGL `compileProgram()` accepts `spec.source`, `spec.glsl`, or
`spec.fragment`, plus optional `spec.vertex`. WebGPU accepts `spec.wgsl`, then
generic non-GLSL `source`, then non-GLSL `fragment`. WebGPU detects entry points
from annotations and uses `entryPoint`/specific entry-point fields where
provided.

Pixel parity request:

```ts
testPixelParity({
  effect_id?: string,
  effects?: string,  // CSV, not expanded glob syntax
  epsilon?: number,  // default 1; no runtime range validation
  seed?: number      // default 42
})
```

The tool selects WebGL2, waits for a noncompiling graph, warms six live frames,
captures the offscreen render surface at time zero, then repeats under WebGPU.
It writes the seed to pipeline/pass uniforms. WebGPU rows are vertically flipped
to the same top-down orientation as WebGL before comparison.

Sources: Shade MCP `src/tools/browser/parity.ts` — `testPixelParity()`,
`captureSurface()`.

### Validate

For RGBA8 channel arrays of equal dimensions:

```text
channel_diff       = abs(webgl_channel - webgpu_channel)        // [0,255]
channel_mismatch   = channel_diff > epsilon
max_diff           = max(channel_diff)                           // [0,255]
mean_diff          = sum(channel_diff) / channel_count           // [0,255]
mismatch_count     = count(channel_mismatch)                      // [0,channel_count]
mismatch_percent   = 100 * mismatch_count / channel_count        // [0,100]
status             = "ok" iff mismatch_percent < 1
```

The required literal contract marker is `mismatch_percent < 1`.

Solid-color diagnostics compute raw 0–255 RGB population variance for each
backend. A backend is solid when all three variances are `< 5`. Variances and
means are rounded to integer arrays in output; the predicate uses unrounded
values.

Y-flip diagnostics compare normal mean absolute difference with a vertically
flipped WebGPU comparison:

```text
y_flip_ratio = y_flip_mean_diff / mean_diff, or 1 when mean_diff === 0
y_flip_detected = mean_diff > 2
                  && y_flip_mean_diff < mean_diff
                  && y_flip_ratio < 0.7
y_flip_clean_flip = mean_diff > 2 && y_flip_ratio < 0.25
```

`epsilon` is described as 0–255 but Zod does not enforce that range. A strict
caller must require `0 <= epsilon <= 255` and a finite value before calling.

Sources: Shade MCP `src/tools/browser/parity.ts` — comparison, solid, and
Y-flip predicates.

### Diagnose

```ts
type ParitySuccessOrMismatch = {
  effect_id: string
  status: "ok" | "mismatch"
  maxDiff: number
  meanDiff: number
  mismatchCount: number
  mismatchPercent: number
  resolution: [number, number]
  glslSolid: boolean
  wgslSolid: boolean
  glslVariance: [number, number, number]
  wgslVariance: [number, number, number]
  yFlipDetected: boolean
  yFlipCleanFlip: boolean
  yFlipMismatchPercent: number
  yFlipMeanDiff: number
  yFlipRatio: number
  issues: string[]
  details: string
  console_errors?: string[]
}

type ParityFailure = {
  effect_id?: string
  status: "error"
  maxDiff?: 0
  meanDiff?: 0
  mismatchCount?: 0
  mismatchPercent?: 0
  resolution?: [number, number]
  details?: string
  error?: string
  console_errors?: string[]
}
```

Enumerated in-tool error details are `Failed to capture WebGL2`, `Failed to
capture WebGPU`, and
`Capture size mismatch: glsl <w>x<h> vs wgsl <w>x<h>`. Compile/browser failures
can instead use the outer `{effect_id,status:"error",error}` shape.

Issues include `GLSL SOLID COLOR (mean=...)`, `WGSL SOLID COLOR (mean=...)`, and
`Y-FLIP`/`PARTIAL Y-FLIP DETECTED (...)`. Solid-color and Y-flip issues do not
change `status`; status depends only on mismatch percentage. A strict gate must
also assert both solid flags false and `yFlipDetected === false` unless the
effect is intentionally solid.

Backend compilation failures:

```ts
// Shared pipeline
{ code: "ERR_PROGRAM_SPEC_MISSING", program: string, pass: string }

// WebGL2
Error("Shader source missing for program '<id>'. You may need to regenerate the shader manifest.")
{ code: "ERR_SHADER_COMPILE", detail: string, source: string }
{ code: "ERR_SHADER_LINK", detail: string, program: string }
{ code: "ERR_UNIFORM_BLOCK_TOO_LARGE", detail: string, program: WebGLProgram }
{ code: "ERR_PROGRAM_NOT_FOUND", pass: string, program: string }
{ code: "ERR_TOO_MANY_TEXTURES", pass: string, limit: number }

// WebGPU
{ code: "ERR_NO_WGSL_SOURCE", detail: string, program: string }
{ code: "ERR_SHADER_COMPILE", detail: string, program: string }
{ code: "ERR_PROGRAM_NOT_FOUND", pass: string, program: string }
{ code: "ERR_TEXTURE_NOT_FOUND", pass: string, texture: string }
{ code: "ERR_NO_MRT_OUTPUTS", pass: string }
{ code: "ERR_COMPUTE_DISPATCH_UNRESOLVED", pass: string, detail: string }
```

Sources: backend compile/execute methods; Shade MCP
`src/tools/browser/parity.ts`.

<!-- SURFACE:MCP -->
## Shade MCP tool contracts

### Introspect

Use MCP `initialize`, send `notifications/initialized`, then call `tools/list`.
For the snapshot at the top of this document, `tools/list` returned the 18 verb
names documented below. The transport result for every verb is:

```ts
type McpTextResult = {
  content: Array<{ type: "text", text: string /* JSON.stringify(payload) */ }>
}
```

Decode `JSON.parse(result.content.find(x => x.type === "text").text)` before
applying the payload types below. These tools do not declare MCP structured
content/output schemas; JSON is carried as text.

The repository config supplies:

```text
SHADE_EFFECTS_DIR=./shaders/effects
SHADE_VIEWER_ROOT=.
SHADE_VIEWER_PATH=/demo/shaders/
SHADE_GLOBALS_PREFIX=__noisemaker
```

Sources: `.mcp.json`; `.codex/config.toml`; Shade MCP `src/index.ts` — all
registration calls; `src/config.ts`.

### Act

Shared input and batch behavior:

```ts
type EffectSelector = {
  effect_id?: string
  effects?: string // comma-separated IDs; takes precedence over effect_id
}

type BrowserSelector = EffectSelector & {
  backend?: "webgl2" | "webgpu" // defaults to webgl2 where present
}

type Batched<T> = T | T[] // object for exactly one selected ID, array otherwise
type OuterEffectError = { effect_id: string, status: "error", error: string }
```

`effects` is split on commas and trimmed. Despite some tool descriptions, it is
not glob-expanded. With neither selector, `resolveEffectIds()` scans
`SHADE_EFFECTS_DIR`: one discovered effect is selected, multiple effects cause
an error, and a flat effect directory uses its basename. See GAP-018.

No numeric schema below uses Zod `.int()`, `.finite()`, `.min()`, `.max()`, or a
positive-resolution refinement unless explicitly stated. Descriptive ranges
are not validation.

#### compileEffect

```ts
type CompileEffectInput = EffectSelector & {
  backend?: "webgl2" | "webgpu" // default webgl2
}

type CompileEffectResult = Batched<
  | { effect_id: string, status: "ok" | "error", backend: string,
      passes: Array<{ id: string, status: "ok" | "error",
                      errors?: string[] }>,
      message: string, console_errors?: string[] }
  | OuterEffectError
>
```

The tool selects the effect and polls status text for up to 300000 ms. On
success it projects expanded passes to `{id:p.name || "pass_<index>",status}`;
expanded passes normally have no `name`, so `pass_<index>` is expected. Success
message is `Compiled successfully`; timeout message is `Compile timeout`.

The status text can still describe the previous effect immediately after the
selection event. Unlike `testPixelParity`, this tool does not wait for
`pipeline.isCompiling === false` and a nonempty current graph. Its returned
passes can therefore belong to the previous graph while status is `ok`. Do not
accept the pass list unless an independent current-effect/graph identity check
agrees. This is GAP-024.

Source: Shade MCP `src/tools/browser/compile.ts` — `compileEffectSchema`,
`compileEffect()`.

#### renderEffectFrame

```ts
type RenderEffectFrameInput = BrowserSelector & {
  warmup_frames?: number                 // default 10
  capture_image?: boolean                // default false
  uniforms?: Record<string, number>
  time?: number                          // seconds; current deadlock risk
  resolution?: [number, number]
}

type RenderEffectFrameResult = Batched<RenderSuccess | RenderFailure>
```

Metric definitions, ranges, and thresholds are in Rendered output. A captured
image is a `data:image/png;base64,...` URI; otherwise `image_uri` is null.

Source: Shade MCP `src/tools/browser/render.ts` —
`renderEffectFrameSchema`, `renderEffectFrame()`.

#### describeEffectFrame

```ts
type DescribeEffectFrameInput = EffectSelector & {
  prompt: string
  backend?: "webgl2" | "webgpu" // default webgl2
}

type DescribeEffectFrameResult = Batched<
  | { effect_id: string, status: "ok",
      frame: { image_uri: string },
      vision: JsonValue | null }
  | { effect_id: string, status: "error", error: string }
>
```

The intended AI JSON is `{description,tags,notes}`, but successful `JSON.parse`
output is not schema-checked and can be any `JsonValue`. If parsing throws, the
fallback is `{description:<raw text>,tags:[],notes:null}`. Failure strings
include the common no-key message and `Failed to render frame`.

Source: Shade MCP `src/tools/browser/describe.ts` — schema and
`describeEffectFrame()`; `src/ai/provider.ts` — provider/no-key behavior.

#### benchmarkEffectFPS

```ts
type BenchmarkEffectFPSInput = BrowserSelector & {
  target_fps?: number       // default 60
  duration_seconds?: number // default 5
  resolution?: [number, number]
}

type BenchmarkEffectFPSResult = Batched<
  | { effect_id: string, status: "ok", backend: string,
      achieved_fps: number,
      meets_target: boolean,
      stats: {
        frame_count: number,
        avg_frame_time_ms: number,
        jitter_ms: number,
        min_frame_time_ms: number,
        max_frame_time_ms: number
      }, console_errors?: string[] }
  | OuterEffectError
>
```

`achieved_fps = frame_count/(sum(frame_times_ms)/1000)` and
`meets_target = achieved_fps >= target_fps`; the literal contract marker is
`fps >= target_fps`. Jitter is sample standard deviation with divisor
`frame_count-1`, or zero for at most one frame. Reported values are rounded to
two decimals. Tool status remains `ok` when `meets_target` is false; callers
must inspect the boolean.

Source: Shade MCP `src/tools/browser/benchmark.ts` — schema and
`benchmarkEffectFPS()`.

#### testUniformResponsiveness

```ts
type TestUniformResponsivenessInput = BrowserSelector
type TestUniformResponsivenessResult = Batched<
  | ({ effect_id: string } & UniformResponsiveness)
  | OuterEffectError
>
```

The test set, perturbation rule, `luma_delta > 0.002` and
`max_channel_delta > 0.002` thresholds, and overall-status weakness are in
Parameters and globals.

Source: Shade MCP `src/tools/browser/uniforms.ts` — schema and
`testUniformResponsiveness()`.

#### testNoPassthrough

```ts
type TestNoPassthroughInput = BrowserSelector

type TestNoPassthroughResult = Batched<
  | { effect_id: string, status: "ok" | "passthrough",
      isFilterEffect: true,
      temporalDiff: number,       // normalized [0,1]
      uniqueColors: number,
      details: "Effect modifies input" |
               "Effect may be passing through unchanged",
      console_errors?: string[] }
  | { effect_id: string, status: "skipped" | "error",
      isFilterEffect: false, similarity: null, details: string,
      console_errors?: string[] }
  | OuterEffectError
>
```

The tool classifies an effect as a filter only when an expanded pass input value
contains the substring `input`. It captures WebGL frames at render times 0 and
1, samples with the same approximately-1000-pixel stride, and computes average
absolute RGB channel difference divided by 255. It returns `ok` when
`temporalDiff > 0.01` OR `uniqueColors > 5`. These are the literal returned
metric names. Nonfilters return `Not a filter effect`.
In-page failures include `No effect loaded` and `No GL context`.

This test does not compare output to input; it compares output at two times and
color cardinality. A static varied passthrough can pass. See GAP-019.

Source: Shade MCP `src/tools/browser/passthrough.ts` — schema and
`testNoPassthrough()`.

#### testPixelParity

```ts
type TestPixelParityInput = EffectSelector & {
  epsilon?: number // default 1, described 0..255 but not range-checked
  seed?: number    // default 42
}

type TestPixelParityResult = Batched<
  ParitySuccessOrMismatch | ParityFailure | OuterEffectError
>
```

All output fields and predicates are in Cross-backend parity.

Source: Shade MCP `src/tools/browser/parity.ts` — schema and
`testPixelParity()`.

#### runDslProgram

```ts
type RunDslProgramInput = {
  dsl: string
  backend?: "webgl2" | "webgpu" // default webgl2
  warmup_frames?: number         // default 10
  capture_image?: boolean        // default false
  uniforms?: Record<string, number>
}

type RunDslProgramResult =
  | { status: "ok", backend: string,
      frame: { width: number, height: number, image_uri: string | null },
      metrics: {
        mean_rgb: Vec3,
        unique_sampled_colors: number,
        is_all_zero: boolean,
        is_monochrome: boolean
      }, console_errors?: string[] }
  | { status: "error", error: string, console_errors?: string[] }
```

Compilation attempts the page editor/run button, then renderer `compile()`, and
polls for up to 300000 ms. Enumerated messages are `No DSL editor or renderer
found`, `Compile timeout`, `No renderer`, and `No GL context`, plus page status
or thrown compiler text. It directly requires `pipeline.backend.gl`, so a
WebGPU request cannot reach success. It does not return alpha, standard
deviation, luma variance, transparency, or low-luma-variance metrics.

Source: Shade MCP `src/tools/browser/dsl.ts` — schema and `runDslProgram()`.

#### checkEffectStructure

```ts
type CheckEffectStructureInput = { effect_id: string }

type CheckEffectStructureResult =
  | { status: "ok" | "warning",
      unusedFiles: string[],
      namingIssues: Array<{ type: "func" | "global", name: string,
                            reason: "Must be camelCase" }>,
      nameCollisions: Array<{
        type: "uniform_function" | "reserved_word" | "builtin_shadow",
        name: string, file: string, message: string
      }>,
      leakedInternalUniforms: string[],
      missingDescription: boolean,
      structuralParityIssues: Array<{
        type: "missing_wgsl" | "missing_glsl", program: string, message: string
      }>,
      requiredUniformIssues: unknown[],
      multiPass: boolean,
      passCount: number }
  | { status: "error", error: string }
```

Status is warning when any unused file, naming issue, name collision, leaked
internal uniform, missing description, or structural parity issue exists.
`requiredUniformIssues` is initialized but never populated or included in the
status predicate. Pass `name` values are not checked. GLSL reserved/builtin
collision checks use regex-extracted declarations.

Source: Shade MCP `src/tools/analysis/structure.ts` —
`checkEffectStructure()`.

#### checkAlgEquiv

```ts
type CheckAlgEquivInput = { effect_id: string }

type CheckAlgEquivResult =
  | { status: "ok" | "divergent",
      pairs: Array<{
        program: string,
        parity: "equivalent" | "divergent" | "error" | string,
        confidence?: "high" | "medium" | "low" | string,
        notes?: string,
        concerns?: string[],
        [key: string]: unknown
      }>,
      unmatchedGlsl?: string[],
      unmatchedWgsl?: string[],
      summary: string }
  | { status: "error", error: string }
```

Only same-stem `.glsl` and `.wgsl` files are paired; split `.vert`/`.frag` files
are ignored. AI JSON is not schema-validated. Overall divergence counts only
entries whose `parity` is exactly `divergent`; AI parse/error entries can leave
overall status `ok`. Errors include the no-key message, `Missing glsl/ or wgsl/
directory`, and `No matching GLSL/WGSL pairs found`.

Source: Shade MCP `src/tools/analysis/alg-equiv.ts` — `checkAlgEquiv()`.

#### compareShaders

```ts
type CompareShadersInput = { effect_id: string }

type ShaderStructure = {
  lines: number
  functions: string[]
  uniforms: string[]
}

type CompareShadersResult = {
  status: "ok"
  programs: Array<
    | { program: string, glsl: ShaderStructure, wgsl: ShaderStructure,
        lineDiff: number, functionCountDiff: number }
    | { program: string, glsl: ShaderStructure | null,
        wgsl: ShaderStructure | null,
        note: "No WGSL counterpart" | "No GLSL counterpart" }
  >
  summary: string
}
```

Only `.glsl` and `.wgsl` files are scanned. Function/uniform detection uses
regex after comment stripping. This tool always returns status `ok`, including
missing counterparts; inspect `note` and null sides.

Source: Shade MCP `src/tools/analysis/compare.ts` — extractors and
`compareShaders()`.

#### analyzeBranching

```ts
type AnalyzeBranchingInput = {
  effect_id: string
  backend?: "webgl2" | "webgpu" // default webgl2
}

type AnalyzeBranchingResult =
  | ({ status: JsonValue } & Record<string, JsonValue>)
  | { status: "error", error: string }
```

GLSL mode reads `.glsl`; WebGPU reads `.wgsl`. AI output is not schema-
validated. The handler first computes `warning` when the sum of all returned
opportunity-array lengths is at least two and `ok` otherwise, irrespective of
severity. It then spreads parsed AI JSON after that field. An AI object with a
`status` member therefore overwrites the computed value with any JSON value;
callers must validate `shaders` themselves and recompute the opportunity count.
This is GAP-025. Errors before the AI call use `status:"error"` and include no
AI key, missing shader directory, and no shader files.

Source: Shade MCP `src/tools/analysis/branching.ts` —
`analyzeBranching()`.

#### searchEffects

```ts
type SearchEffectsInput = { query: string, limit?: number /* default 10 */ }
type SearchEffectsResult = {
  query: string
  results: Array<{ id: string, description: string,
                   tags: string[], score: number }>
  total: number
}
```

The query is lowercased, synonym-expanded, and whitespace-tokenized. Score is
an unbounded additive relevance number: full ID substring +20; per keyword ID
+8, name +15, description +5, tag +8, namespace +12. Only positive scores are
returned, descending, sliced to `limit`.

Source: Shade MCP `src/tools/knowledge/search-effects.ts`;
`src/knowledge/effect-index.ts` — `search()`.

#### analyzeEffect

```ts
type AnalyzeEffectInput = { effect_id: string }
type AnalyzeEffectResult =
  | { effectId: string, name?: string, description?: string,
      namespace?: string, tags: string[],
      globals: Record<string, unknown>, passes: Array<Record<string, unknown>>,
      format: "js" | "json",
      shaders: Record<`glsl/${string}.glsl` | `wgsl/${string}.wgsl`, string> }
  | { error: string }
```

For `definition.js`, Shade uses a regex/balanced-brace projection rather than
importing the live class. It extracts only globals that contain a `uniform`, a
limited set of scalar fields, and pass `program` names; it does not reproduce
the full Noisemaker definition. Shader reads ignore split `.vert`/`.frag` files.
Use this tool for discovery, not full definition introspection. See GAP-017.

Source: Shade MCP `src/tools/knowledge/analyze-effect.ts`;
`src/formats/definition-js.ts` — `parseDefinitionJs()`.

#### searchShaderSource

```ts
type SearchShaderSourceInput = {
  query: string
  context_lines?: number // default 5
  limit?: number         // default 10
}
type SearchShaderSourceResult = {
  query: string
  matchCount: number
  results: Array<{
    effectId: string, file: string, lineNumber: number,
    matchLine: string, context: string
  }>
}
```

Only `.glsl` files are indexed. `query` is compiled case-insensitively as a
regular expression; invalid regex syntax is escaped and treated literally.
Context line numbers are one-based. After a match, nearby lines equal to the
context window are skipped.

Source: Shade MCP `src/tools/knowledge/search-source.ts`;
`src/knowledge/glsl-index.ts` — `search()`.

#### searchShaderKnowledge

```ts
type SearchShaderKnowledgeInput = {
  query: string
  category?: string
  limit?: number // default 5
}
type SearchShaderKnowledgeResult = {
  query: string
  category: string
  matchCount: number
  databaseStats: {
    totalDocuments: number, totalTerms: number, indexed: boolean,
```
