```ts
    categories: Record<string, number>
  }
  results: Array<{
    title: string, category: string, score: number,
    snippet: string, tags?: string[], content?: string
  }>
}
```

Search uses synonym expansion and TF-IDF cosine similarity. Returned score is
rounded to three decimals and must be at least `0.05`; cosine similarity is in
`[0,1]`. Full `content` is included only for the first three results.

Source: Shade MCP `src/tools/knowledge/search-knowledge.ts`;
`src/knowledge/vector-db.ts` — `search()`, `getStats()`.

#### listEffects

```ts
type ListEffectsInput = { namespace?: string }
type ListEffectsResult = {
  namespace: string
  count: number
  effects: Array<{
    id: string, name: string | undefined, description: string,
    tags: string[], passes: number
  }>
}
```

Without a filter, `namespace` in output is `all`. The list uses Shade's lossy
definition projection; pass counts and names reflect that parser, not imported
Noisemaker class instances.

Source: Shade MCP `src/tools/utility/list-effects.ts`;
`src/knowledge/effect-index.ts` — `list()`.

#### generateManifest

```ts
type GenerateManifestInput = Record<string, unknown> // schema has no properties and no additionalProperties:false; handler ignores args
type GenerateManifestResult =
  | { status: "ok", path: string, effectCount: number }
  | { error: string }
```

This verb writes `<SHADE_EFFECTS_DIR>/manifest.json` immediately with compact,
sorted JSON. It scans definition source with regexes for description, external
texture/mesh, tags, surface globals, and starter heuristics, and scans shader
files. It is state-changing; do not call during read-only introspection. The
only explicit error is `Effects directory not found: <path>`; filesystem write
exceptions escape the handler.

Source: Shade MCP `src/tools/utility/generate-manifest.ts` —
`registerGenerateManifest()`.

### Validate

A tool call is protocol-valid when the MCP result is not an MCP `error` and its
text content parses as JSON. That is not domain success. Apply the verb-specific
predicate:

| Verb | Domain pass predicate |
|---|---|
| `compileEffect` | every result `status === "ok"` and every pass status `ok` |
| `renderEffectFrame` | status `ok`, then output metrics/harness exemptions |
| `describeEffectFrame` | status `ok` and required application-specific vision fields present |
| `benchmarkEffectFPS` | status `ok` and `meets_target === true` |
| `testUniformResponsiveness` | status `ok` and every entry ends `:pass` |
| `testNoPassthrough` | `ok`, or `skipped` only for a confirmed nonfilter |
| `testPixelParity` | status `ok`, no disallowed solid/Y-flip flags |
| `runDslProgram` | status `ok`, then its limited metrics pass |
| `checkEffectStructure` | status `ok` and all issue arrays empty |
| `checkAlgEquiv` | status `ok`, every pair exactly `equivalent`, no unmatched lists |
| `compareShaders` | both sides non-null and no `note` for every program |
| `analyzeBranching` | validate AI JSON, then recompute opportunity count; `>=2` maps to the tool's intended warning, because returned status is overwritable (GAP-025) |
| search/list/analyze | requested record present; these have no success status |
| `generateManifest` | status `ok`, then independently inspect the written diff |

Batch callers must validate every array member. Never treat transport success,
outer `status:"ok"`, or a nonempty result as universal validation.

### Diagnose

Selector/scan failures thrown before per-effect loops include:

```text
Effects directory not found: <dir>. Specify effect_id or set SHADE_EFFECTS_DIR.
Failed to scan effects directory: <dir>
No effects found in <dir>. Specify effect_id.
Multiple effects found (<n>). Please specify effect_id or effects parameter. Available: ...
```

Browser tools usually catch per-effect exceptions as
`OuterEffectError`. Analysis/knowledge tools use several incompatible forms:
`{status:"error",error}`, `{error}`, or unconditional `status:"ok"` plus notes.
No shared machine-readable failure union is declared by the MCP server. See
GAP-020.

AI-dependent tools use this exact no-key message:

```text
No AI API key found. Set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY or OPENAI_API_KEY, or create .anthropic/.openai file in project root.
```

Browser results can append `console_errors:string[]` through
`runWithConsoleCapture`, but field presence depends on the wrapper/result and
the messages are not categorized. Tool-specific failures are enumerated under
each verb above.

For `compileEffect`, `renderEffectFrame`, `describeEffectFrame`,
`benchmarkEffectFPS`, `testUniformResponsiveness`, `testNoPassthrough`, and
`runDslProgram`, a domain-shaped `ok` response can still be stale. Six wait on
status text directly; `describeEffectFrame` delegates to `renderEffectFrame`.
No returned field identifies that failure, so graph/effect identity
disagreement must be diagnosed outside the returned contract. This is
GAP-024. `testPixelParity` uses the current `pipeline.isCompiling` signal and
does not share this specific race.

Sources: Shade MCP `src/tools/resolve-effects.ts`; all tool registration files;
`src/ai/provider.ts` — `NO_AI_KEY_MESSAGE`;
`src/harness/browser-session.ts` — console capture.

## Fully worked, source-complete, validated effect

This instance is the repository's `synth/testPattern` effect. The three source
blocks below are complete, not excerpts. The MCP evidence was captured against
Noisemaker commit `755071128ba6112753b9e976dc0ebbb8e55449b5` and Shade MCP
commit `7fd0d9758e64ec587950a49496b31eb59ae17857`. MCP request JSON is shown in
wire order. Each response is the JSON value decoded from the server's
`content[0].text` field.

### Authoring inputs

<!-- WORKED:DEFINITION -->

`shaders/effects/synth/testPattern/definition.js`:

```js
import { Effect } from '../../../src/runtime/effect.js'

export default new Effect({
  name: "Test Pattern",
  namespace: "synth",
  func: "testPattern",
  tags: ["util"],

  description: "Test patterns for debugging and calibration",
  globals: {
    pattern: {
      type: "int",
      default: 0,
      uniform: "pattern",
      choices: { checkerboard: 0, colorBars: 1, gradient: 2, uvMap: 3, gridLines: 4, colorGrid: 5, dotGrid: 6 },
      ui: { label: "pattern", control: "dropdown" }
    },
    gridSize: {
      type: "int",
      default: 4,
      min: 1,
      max: 16,
      uniform: "gridSize",
      ui: {
        label: "grid size",
        control: "slider",
        enabledBy: { param: "pattern", in: [0, 4, 5, 6] }
      }
    }
  },
  passes: [
    {
      name: "main",
      program: "testPattern",
      inputs: {},
      outputs: {
        color: "outputTex"
      }
    }
  ]
})
```

<!-- WORKED:GLSL -->

`shaders/effects/synth/testPattern/glsl/testPattern.glsl`:

```glsl
#version 300 es
precision highp float;

uniform vec2 resolution;
uniform vec2 tileOffset;
uniform vec2 fullResolution;
uniform int gridSize;
uniform int pattern;

out vec4 fragColor;

// 3x5 pixel font for digits 0-9
// Each digit is encoded as 15 bits (3 columns x 5 rows, row-major)
const int GLYPH[10] = int[10](
    0x7B6F,  // 0: 111 101 101 101 111
    0x2492,  // 1: 010 010 010 010 010
    0x73E7,  // 2: 111 001 111 100 111
    0x72CF,  // 3: 111 001 011 001 111
    0x5BC9,  // 4: 101 101 111 001 001
    0x79CF,  // 5: 111 100 111 001 111
    0x79EF,  // 6: 111 100 111 101 111
    0x7249,  // 7: 111 001 001 001 001
    0x7BEF,  // 8: 111 101 111 101 111
    0x7BCF   // 9: 111 101 111 001 111
);

// Sample a glyph at local coordinates (0-2, 0-4)
bool sampleGlyph(int digit, int x, int y) {
    if (digit < 0 || digit > 9 || x < 0 || x > 2 || y < 0 || y > 4) return false;
    int bitIndex = y * 3 + (2 - x);  // row-major, top-left origin
    return ((GLYPH[digit] >> bitIndex) & 1) == 1;
}

// Render a number at a position within a cell
bool renderNumber(int number, vec2 cellUV) {
    // Determine how many digits we need
    int numDigits = 1;
    if (number >= 10) numDigits = 2;
    if (number >= 100) numDigits = 3;

    // Glyph dimensions in UV space (centered, cell-local)
    float glyphWidth = 0.15;
    float glyphHeight = 0.35;
    float spacing = 0.05;

    float totalWidth = float(numDigits) * glyphWidth + float(numDigits - 1) * spacing;
    float startX = 0.5 - totalWidth * 0.5;
    float startY = 0.5 - glyphHeight * 0.5;

    // Check if we're in the vertical range for glyphs
    if (cellUV.y < startY || cellUV.y >= startY + glyphHeight) return false;

    // Extract digits (right to left)
    int digits[3];
    int temp = number;
    for (int i = 0; i < 3; i++) {
        digits[i] = temp % 10;
        temp /= 10;
    }

    // Check each digit position (left to right)
    for (int d = 0; d < numDigits; d++) {
        float digitX = startX + float(d) * (glyphWidth + spacing);

        if (cellUV.x >= digitX && cellUV.x < digitX + glyphWidth) {
            // We're in this digit's horizontal range
            float localX = (cellUV.x - digitX) / glyphWidth;
            float localY = (cellUV.y - startY) / glyphHeight;

            // Map to 3x5 grid
            int gx = int(localX * 3.0);
            int gy = int(localY * 5.0);

            // Get the correct digit (numDigits-1-d because digits[] is reversed)
            int digit = digits[numDigits - 1 - d];

            return sampleGlyph(digit, gx, gy);
        }
    }

    return false;
}

// Pattern 0: Numbered checkerboard
vec4 checkerboard(vec2 uv) {
    int n = max(gridSize, 1);
    int cellX = int(uv.x * float(n)) % n;
    int cellY = int(uv.y * float(n)) % n;

    int cellNum = (n - 1 - cellY) * n + cellX;

    bool isWhiteCell = ((cellX + cellY) % 2) == 0;

    vec2 cellUV = fract(uv * float(n));

    bool isGlyph = renderNumber(cellNum, cellUV);

    float cellColor = isWhiteCell ? 1.0 : 0.0;
    float glyphColor = isWhiteCell ? 0.0 : 1.0;
    float finalColor = isGlyph ? glyphColor : cellColor;

    return vec4(vec3(finalColor), 1.0);
}

// Pattern 1: 8 vertical SMPTE-style color bars
vec4 colorBars(vec2 uv) {
    int bar = int(uv.x * 8.0);
    bar = clamp(bar, 0, 7);

    // white, yellow, cyan, green, magenta, red, blue, black
    vec3 colors[8] = vec3[8](
        vec3(1.0, 1.0, 1.0),
        vec3(1.0, 1.0, 0.0),
        vec3(0.0, 1.0, 1.0),
        vec3(0.0, 1.0, 0.0),
        vec3(1.0, 0.0, 1.0),
        vec3(1.0, 0.0, 0.0),
        vec3(0.0, 0.0, 1.0),
        vec3(0.0, 0.0, 0.0)
    );

    return vec4(colors[bar], 1.0);
}

// Pattern 2: Horizontal black-to-white gradient ramp
vec4 gradient(vec2 uv) {
    return vec4(vec3(uv.x), 1.0);
}

// Pattern 3: UV map (R=u, G=v, B=0)
vec4 uvMap(vec2 uv) {
    return vec4(uv.x, uv.y, 0.0, 1.0);
}

// Pattern 4: Thin white grid lines on black
vec4 gridLines(vec2 uv) {
    int n = max(gridSize, 1);
    vec2 cellUV = fract(uv * float(n));
    vec2 edge = min(cellUV, 1.0 - cellUV);

    // Use direct calculation instead of fwidth() for tile-aware rendering.
    // This maintains the same line thickness in normal rendering while ensuring
    // continuity across tile boundaries during large-format print export.
    vec2 fw = vec2(1.0) / fullResolution * float(n);

    float line = 1.0 - smoothstep(0.0, 2.0 * fw.x, edge.x) * smoothstep(0.0, 2.0 * fw.y, edge.y);
    return vec4(vec3(line), 1.0);
}

// HSV to RGB (hue only, full saturation & value)
vec3 hue2rgb(float h) {
    float r = abs(h * 6.0 - 3.0) - 1.0;
    float g = 2.0 - abs(h * 6.0 - 2.0);
    float b = 2.0 - abs(h * 6.0 - 4.0);
    return clamp(vec3(r, g, b), 0.0, 1.0);
}

// Pattern 5: Each cell gets a unique hue
vec4 colorGrid(vec2 uv) {
    int n = max(gridSize, 1);
    int cellX = int(uv.x * float(n)) % n;
    int cellY = int(uv.y * float(n)) % n;
    int cellIndex = cellY * n + cellX;
    float hue = fract(float(cellIndex) * 0.618033988749895);
    return vec4(hue2rgb(hue), 1.0);
}

// Pattern 6: Filled circle at each grid intersection
vec4 dotGrid(vec2 uv) {
    int n = max(gridSize, 1);
    vec2 scaled = uv * float(n);
    vec2 nearest = round(scaled);
    float dist = length(scaled - nearest);
    float dot = 1.0 - smoothstep(0.12, 0.15, dist);
    return vec4(vec3(dot), 1.0);
}

void main() {
    vec2 globalCoord = gl_FragCoord.xy + tileOffset;
    vec2 uv = globalCoord / fullResolution;

    if (pattern == 1) {
        fragColor = colorBars(uv);
    } else if (pattern == 2) {
        fragColor = gradient(uv);
    } else if (pattern == 3) {
        fragColor = uvMap(uv);
    } else if (pattern == 4) {
        fragColor = gridLines(uv);
    } else if (pattern == 5) {
        fragColor = colorGrid(uv);
    } else if (pattern == 6) {
        fragColor = dotGrid(uv);
    } else {
        fragColor = checkerboard(uv);
    }
}
```

<!-- WORKED:WGSL -->

`shaders/effects/synth/testPattern/wgsl/testPattern.wgsl`:

```wgsl
// WGSL version – WebGPU

struct Uniforms {
    resolution: vec2f,
    gridSize: i32,
    pattern: i32,
    tileOffset: vec2f,
    fullResolution: vec2f,
}

@group(0) @binding(0) var<uniform> uniforms: Uniforms;

// 3x5 pixel font for digits 0-9
// Each digit is encoded as 15 bits (3 columns x 5 rows, row-major)
const GLYPH = array<i32, 10>(
    0x7B6F,  // 0: 111 101 101 101 111
    0x2492,  // 1: 010 010 010 010 010
    0x73E7,  // 2: 111 001 111 100 111
    0x72CF,  // 3: 111 001 011 001 111
    0x5BC9,  // 4: 101 101 111 001 001
    0x79CF,  // 5: 111 100 111 001 111
    0x79EF,  // 6: 111 100 111 101 111
    0x7249,  // 7: 111 001 001 001 001
    0x7BEF,  // 8: 111 101 111 101 111
    0x7BCF   // 9: 111 101 111 001 111
);

// Sample a glyph at local coordinates (0-2, 0-4)
fn sampleGlyph(digit: i32, x: i32, y: i32) -> bool {
    if (digit < 0 || digit > 9 || x < 0 || x > 2 || y < 0 || y > 4) {
        return false;
    }
    let bitIndex = y * 3 + (2 - x);  // row-major, top-left origin
    return ((GLYPH[digit] >> u32(bitIndex)) & 1) == 1;
}

// Render a number at a position within a cell
fn renderNumber(number: i32, cellUV: vec2f) -> bool {
    // Determine how many digits we need
    var numDigits = 1;
    if (number >= 10) { numDigits = 2; }
    if (number >= 100) { numDigits = 3; }

    // Glyph dimensions in UV space (centered, scaled to fit nicely)
    let glyphWidth = 0.15;
    let glyphHeight = 0.35;
    let spacing = 0.05;

    let totalWidth = f32(numDigits) * glyphWidth + f32(numDigits - 1) * spacing;
    let startX = 0.5 - totalWidth * 0.5;
    let startY = 0.5 - glyphHeight * 0.5;

    // Check if we're in the vertical range for glyphs
    if (cellUV.y < startY || cellUV.y >= startY + glyphHeight) {
        return false;
    }

    // Extract digits (right to left)
    var digits = array<i32, 3>(0, 0, 0);
    var temp = number;
    for (var i = 0; i < 3; i++) {
        digits[i] = temp % 10;
        temp = temp / 10;
    }

    // Check each digit position (left to right)
    for (var d = 0; d < numDigits; d++) {
        let digitX = startX + f32(d) * (glyphWidth + spacing);

        if (cellUV.x >= digitX && cellUV.x < digitX + glyphWidth) {
            // We're in this digit's horizontal range
            let localX = (cellUV.x - digitX) / glyphWidth;
            let localY = (cellUV.y - startY) / glyphHeight;

            // Map to 3x5 grid
            let gx = i32(localX * 3.0);
            let gy = i32(localY * 5.0);

            // Get the correct digit (numDigits-1-d because digits[] is reversed)
            let digit = digits[numDigits - 1 - d];

            return sampleGlyph(digit, gx, gy);
        }
    }

    return false;
}

// Pattern 0: Numbered checkerboard
fn checkerboard(uv: vec2f) -> vec4f {
    let n = max(uniforms.gridSize, 1);
    let cellX = i32(uv.x * f32(n)) % n;
    let cellY = i32(uv.y * f32(n)) % n;

    let cellNum = (n - 1 - cellY) * n + cellX;

    let isWhiteCell = ((cellX + cellY) % 2) == 0;

    let cellUV = fract(uv * f32(n));

    let isGlyph = renderNumber(cellNum, cellUV);

    let cellColor = select(0.0, 1.0, isWhiteCell);
    let glyphColor = select(1.0, 0.0, isWhiteCell);
    let finalColor = select(cellColor, glyphColor, isGlyph);

    return vec4f(vec3f(finalColor), 1.0);
}

// Pattern 1: 8 vertical SMPTE-style color bars
fn colorBars(uv: vec2f) -> vec4f {
    var bar = i32(uv.x * 8.0);
    bar = clamp(bar, 0, 7);

    // white, yellow, cyan, green, magenta, red, blue, black
    let colors = array<vec3f, 8>(
        vec3f(1.0, 1.0, 1.0),
        vec3f(1.0, 1.0, 0.0),
        vec3f(0.0, 1.0, 1.0),
        vec3f(0.0, 1.0, 0.0),
        vec3f(1.0, 0.0, 1.0),
        vec3f(1.0, 0.0, 0.0),
        vec3f(0.0, 0.0, 1.0),
        vec3f(0.0, 0.0, 0.0)
    );

    return vec4f(colors[bar], 1.0);
}

// Pattern 2: Horizontal black-to-white gradient ramp
fn gradientRamp(uv: vec2f) -> vec4f {
    return vec4f(vec3f(uv.x), 1.0);
}

// Pattern 3: UV map (R=u, G=v, B=0)
fn uvMapPattern(uv: vec2f) -> vec4f {
    return vec4f(uv.x, uv.y, 0.0, 1.0);
}

// Pattern 4: Thin white grid lines on black
fn gridLines(uv: vec2f) -> vec4f {
    let n = max(uniforms.gridSize, 1);
    let cellUV = fract(uv * f32(n));
    let edge = min(cellUV, 1.0 - cellUV);
    // Non-tiling: original fwidth-based AA (byte-identical baseline).
    // Tiling: analytic AA width mirroring glsl/testPattern.glsl, which is
    // seam-stable across tiles where screen-space derivatives are not.
    let isTile = length(uniforms.tileOffset) > 0.0;
    // fwidthFine must be evaluated in uniform control flow (function scope),
    // so compute it unconditionally, then override only when tiling. The
    // analytic-width divide is skipped entirely on the non-tile path.
    var fw = fwidthFine(uv * f32(n));
    var edgeMul = 1.5;
    if (isTile) {
        let fr = select(uniforms.resolution, uniforms.fullResolution, uniforms.fullResolution.x > 0.0);
        fw = vec2f(1.0) / fr * f32(n);
        edgeMul = 2.0;
    }
    let line = 1.0 - smoothstep(0.0, edgeMul * fw.x, edge.x) * smoothstep(0.0, edgeMul * fw.y, edge.y);
    return vec4f(vec3f(line), 1.0);
}

// HSV to RGB (hue only, full saturation & value)
fn hue2rgb(h: f32) -> vec3f {
    let r = abs(h * 6.0 - 3.0) - 1.0;
    let g = 2.0 - abs(h * 6.0 - 2.0);
    let b = 2.0 - abs(h * 6.0 - 4.0);
    return clamp(vec3f(r, g, b), vec3f(0.0), vec3f(1.0));
}

// Pattern 5: Each cell gets a unique hue
fn colorGrid(uv: vec2f) -> vec4f {
    let n = max(uniforms.gridSize, 1);
    let cellX = i32(uv.x * f32(n)) % n;
    let cellY = i32(uv.y * f32(n)) % n;
    let cellIndex = cellY * n + cellX;
    let hue = fract(f32(cellIndex) * 0.618033988749895);
    return vec4f(hue2rgb(hue), 1.0);
}

// Pattern 6: Filled circle at each grid intersection
fn dotGrid(uv: vec2f) -> vec4f {
    let n = max(uniforms.gridSize, 1);
    let scaled = uv * f32(n);
    let nearest = round(scaled);
    let dist = length(scaled - nearest);
    let d = 1.0 - smoothstep(0.12, 0.15, dist);
    return vec4f(vec3f(d), 1.0);
}

@fragment
fn main(@builtin(position) position: vec4f) -> @location(0) vec4f {
    // Tile-aware global UV (mirror glsl/testPattern.glsl). Non-tiling
    // (tileOffset=(0,0), fullResolution=resolution) is byte-identical.
    let fr = select(uniforms.resolution, uniforms.fullResolution, uniforms.fullResolution.x > 0.0);
    let uv = (position.xy + uniforms.tileOffset) / fr;

    if (uniforms.pattern == 1) {
        return colorBars(uv);
    } else if (uniforms.pattern == 2) {
        return gradientRamp(uv);
    } else if (uniforms.pattern == 3) {
        return uvMapPattern(uv);
    } else if (uniforms.pattern == 4) {
        return gridLines(uv);
    } else if (uniforms.pattern == 5) {
        return colorGrid(uv);
    } else if (uniforms.pattern == 6) {
        return dotGrid(uv);
    } else {
        return checkerboard(uv);
    }
}
```

The legal DSL invocation uses the generated enum identifier, writes the effect
chain to `o0`, and explicitly renders it:

```noisemaker
search synth
testPattern(pattern: checkerboard, gridSize: 4).write(o0)
render(o0)
```

`pattern` is a DSL `Identifier`, not the string literal `"checkerboard"`.
`gridSize` is a numeric expression. A direct `compileGraph()` call registers
the checked-in effect and expands this exact program to two expanded passes:

```json
[
  {"id":"node_0_pass_0","program":"node_0_testPattern","inputs":{},"outputs":{"color":"node_0_out"}},
  {"id":"node_1_write_blit","program":"blit","type":"render","inputs":{"src":"node_0_out"},"outputs":{"color":"global_o0"}}
]
```

The first is the one declared effect pass; the second is the terminal write
blit. This two-pass shape is asserted by
`shaders/tests/test_llms_full_contract.mjs` against the runtime compiler.

Sources: `shaders/effects/synth/testPattern/definition.js` and its complete
GLSL/WGSL program files; `shaders/src/renderer/canvas.js` —
`registerEffectWithRuntime()` enum export; `shaders/src/runtime/expander.js` —
program expansion.

### Exact MCP validation transcript

<!-- WORKED:MCP-REQUESTS -->

Initialization used protocol `2025-06-18`; the following eight calls then ran
in a single server session. The response block is preserved literally. The two
`compileEffect` responses are evidence of GAP-024, not accepted validation:
their three passes came from the previous viewer graph, while the exact DSL
above compiles to two.

```json
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":3,"method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"checkEffectStructure","arguments":{"effect_id":"synth/testPattern"}}}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":4,"method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"compileEffect","arguments":{"effect_id":"synth/testPattern","backend":"webgl2"}}}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":5,"method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"compileEffect","arguments":{"effect_id":"synth/testPattern","backend":"webgpu"}}}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":6,"method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"renderEffectFrame","arguments":{"effect_id":"synth/testPattern","backend":"webgl2","warmup_frames":10,"capture_image":false,"resolution":[256,256]}}}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":7,"method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"testUniformResponsiveness","arguments":{"effect_id":"synth/testPattern","backend":"webgl2"}}}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":8,"method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"testNoPassthrough","arguments":{"effect_id":"synth/testPattern","backend":"webgl2"}}}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":9,"method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"testPixelParity","arguments":{"effect_id":"synth/testPattern","epsilon":1,"seed":42}}}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":10,"method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"benchmarkEffectFPS","arguments":{"effect_id":"synth/testPattern","backend":"webgl2","duration_seconds":1,"target_fps":30,"resolution":[256,256]}}}
```

<!-- WORKED:MCP-RESPONSES -->

Responses, in the same order:

```json
{"status":"ok","unusedFiles":[],"namingIssues":[],"nameCollisions":[],"leakedInternalUniforms":[],"missingDescription":false,"structuralParityIssues":[],"requiredUniformIssues":[],"multiPass":false,"passCount":1}
{"effect_id":"synth/testPattern","status":"ok","passes":[{"id":"pass_0","status":"ok"},{"id":"pass_1","status":"ok"},{"id":"pass_2","status":"ok"}],"message":"Compiled successfully","backend":"webgl2"}
{"effect_id":"synth/testPattern","status":"ok","passes":[{"id":"pass_0","status":"ok"},{"id":"pass_1","status":"ok"},{"id":"pass_2","status":"ok"}],"message":"Compiled successfully","backend":"webgpu"}
{"effect_id":"synth/testPattern","status":"ok","backend":"WebGL2","frame":{"image_uri":null,"width":90,"height":90},"metrics":{"mean_rgb":[0.49851924975320827,0.49851924975320827,0.49851924975320827],"mean_alpha":1,"std_rgb":[0.49999780737389904,0.49999780737389904,0.49999780737389904],"luma_variance":0.2499978073787063,"unique_sampled_colors":2,"is_all_zero":false,"is_all_transparent":false,"is_essentially_blank":false,"is_monochrome":false}}
{"effect_id":"synth/testPattern","status":"ok","tested_uniforms":["gridSize:pass"],"details":"Uniforms affect output"}
{"effect_id":"synth/testPattern","status":"skipped","isFilterEffect":false,"similarity":null,"details":"Not a filter effect"}
{"effect_id":"synth/testPattern","status":"ok","maxDiff":0,"meanDiff":0,"mismatchCount":0,"mismatchPercent":0,"resolution":[1024,1024],"glslSolid":false,"wgslSolid":false,"glslVariance":[16256,16256,16256],"wgslVariance":[16256,16256,16256],"yFlipDetected":false,"yFlipCleanFlip":false,"yFlipMismatchPercent":70.9,"yFlipMeanDiff":180.79,"yFlipRatio":1,"issues":[],"details":"Pixel parity OK (maxDiff=0, meanDiff=0.00)"}
{"effect_id":"synth/testPattern","status":"ok","backend":"webgl2","achieved_fps":52.63,"meets_target":true,"stats":{"frame_count":52,"avg_frame_time_ms":19,"jitter_ms":18.36,"min_frame_time_ms":9,"max_frame_time_ms":148.3}}
```

Falsifiable interpretation:

- Structure: pass because all issue arrays are empty and description exists.
- Compilation tool: **indeterminate**. Its three-pass results conflict with the
  source-derived two-pass graph and are rejected under GAP-024. Dual-backend
  execution is instead established by the zero-diff `testPixelParity` result,
  whose implementation waits on `pipeline.isCompiling` before each capture.
- Frame metrics: threshold pass because alpha `1 >= 0.01`, luma variance
  `0.2499978073787063 >= 0.0001`, sampled color count `2 > 1`, and no blank,
  transparent, or monochrome flag is set. Freshness is not proved by this verb
  because it shares GAP-024; the checkerboard metrics corroborate, but cannot
  machine-identify, the selected effect. There is no generic `>5` rendered-
  output requirement; that separate predicate belongs only to the filter
  modification tool.
- Uniform report: threshold pass because the only tested mutable uniform is
  `gridSize:pass`. `pattern` is a `choices` global and the tool intentionally
  excludes choices. Freshness remains indeterminate under GAP-024.
- Passthrough report: expected skip because the definition has no `inputTex`
  input and therefore is not a filter. Freshness remains indeterminate under
  GAP-024.
- Parity: pass because `mismatchPercent = 0 < 1`, both solid flags are false,
  and no Y flip is detected.
- Performance report: threshold pass because
  `achieved_fps = 52.63 >= target_fps = 30` and the returned `meets_target`
  agrees. Freshness remains indeterminate under GAP-024.

Thus the source/structure check and zero-diff parity call validate this worked
effect end to end on both backends. The other recorded browser reports show
passing metric predicates but are not promoted to freshness-proof validation;
the server lacks the identity signal required to do so.

The requested frame resolution was `[256,256]`, but the captured canvas was
`90x90`. Treat returned frame dimensions as authoritative and see GAP-021.

Sources: Shade MCP tool implementations and harness helpers cited in the tool
contract surface; the literal transcript above was produced by that server
against the two pinned source commits named at the start of this section.

## Surfaces × capabilities traceability matrix

<!-- TRACEABILITY-MATRIX -->

Every cell points to an executable contract or an explicitly identified gap.

| Surface | Introspect | Act | Validate | Diagnose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| DSL program | `Token`, `ParsedProgram`, `CompiledProgram`, registry keys, `CompiledGraph` | Complete EBNF plus call/read/write/render contracts | parse/unparse/compile and render metric predicates | `SyntaxError`, `Diagnostic`, `CompilationFailure`; GAP-001/002/023 |
| Effect definition | `Effect` fields, registry/manifest, `checkEffectStructure` | `EffectDefinition` typed grammar | validator, structure scan, dual-backend compile | exact validator messages, structural arrays, backend errors; GAP-003/007/026 |
| Parameters and globals | `effect.globals`, transformed `uniforms`, registry params | `GlobalSpec` typed grammar and enum/binding rules | compile reflection and numeric response deltas | transform errors, type/range blind spots; GAP-010/011 |
| Passes and render graph | `Effect.passes`, `CompiledGraph.passes`, `CompiledGraph.textures`, `CompiledGraph.allocations` | `PassSpec` typed grammar and sampler/output binding rules | graph analysis and per-pass compile | unresolved inputs and backend compile/resource failures; GAP-005/006 |
| Textures and surface resources | texture specs plus runtime surface dimensions/formats | `TextureSpec` typed grammar and exact accepted policies | allocation existence, size/format inspection, render metrics | unsupported/missing surface failures and silent fields; GAP-004 |
| Compatibility and mutation | compiled AST, `listSteps`, `getCompatibleReplacements` | typed `replaceEffect` contract | candidate precheck plus compile/render after mutation | compatibility and transform errors; GAP-008/022 |
| Rendered output | frame dimensions and `ImageMetrics` | `RenderEffectFrameInput` plus uniform application | exact blank/monochrome/responsiveness/FPS predicates | metric failures, capture errors, exemptions; GAP-009/014/015/021 |
| Cross-backend parity | source-pair availability and parity report | GLSL ES 3.00/WGSL authoring contracts | epsilon diff, `<1%` mismatch, solid/Y-flip checks | compile/readback/divergence reports; GAP-012/016 |
| Shade MCP tool contracts | `tools/list`, effect scanners, all output types | Full signatures for all 18 verbs | verb-specific domain predicates | selector errors and tool-specific unions; GAP-013/017/018/019/020/024/025 |

## Genuine engine instrumentation gaps

<!-- GAP-REGISTER -->

These are not documentation omissions. Each is behavior the inspected code does
not currently expose or enforce, so an agent cannot obtain the requested
contract without an engine/tool change.

| ID | Missing instrumentation | Source evidence | Agent consequence |
|---|---|---|---|
| GAP-001 | No enforced `o0`–`o7` range | `shaders/src/lang/lexer.js` accepts digit-suffixed refs; `shaders/src/runtime/pipeline.js` — `createSurfaces()` preallocates only `o0`–`o7` | `o8` parses and fails later/indirectly instead of producing a range diagnostic |
| GAP-002 | Parser errors are thrown strings/`SyntaxError` without a stable diagnostic schema; `loc` is not documented as a public result | `shaders/src/lang/lexer.js`, `parser.js`, and `diagnostics.js` | retry code must parse human text and cannot reliably identify spans/codes |
| GAP-003 | `validateEffectDefinition()` validates only a subset of the consumed definition schema | `shaders/src/runtime/effect-validator.js` versus `effect.js` and `expander.js` | invalid globals/pass/texture fields can survive structure validation |
| GAP-004 | Mip and persistent-across-frames controls do not exist; definition-level 3D `filter` and unknown texture fields are discarded | `shaders/src/runtime/compiler.js` — `extractTextureSpecs()`; backend texture creation methods | an agent cannot author/query mip policy, persistence, or 3D nearest filtering, and typos can be ignored |
| GAP-005 | `name`, `conditions`, `viewport`, `clear`, `samplerTypes`, and `type` are not copied into expanded effect passes | `shaders/src/runtime/expander.js` — constructed pass object; `pipeline.js` — `shouldSkipPass()` | authoring these fields has no runtime effect through `definition.js` and emits no diagnostic |
| GAP-006 | Resource allocation/liveness output is analysis-only; renderers do not consume its physical allocation plan | `shaders/src/runtime/resources.js` — `analyzeLiveness()`/`allocateResources()`; `compiler.js` stores `graph.allocations` with no pipeline/backend consumer | an agent cannot query the actual runtime allocation/reuse plan promised by the analyzer |
| GAP-007 | Backend shader/compiler failures are not normalized to one structured diagnostic union | `shaders/src/runtime/backends/webgl2.js` and `webgpu.js`; Shade browser tool wrappers | backend retry logic must parse browser/compiler strings |
| GAP-008 | Replace compatibility checks only target existence plus starter/nonstarter chain position and is not an MCP preflight verb | `shaders/src/lang/transform.js` — `getCompatibleReplacements()` and `replaceEffect()` | arguments, sampler topology, shader availability, types, ranges, passes, outputs, and backend support are not predicted before mutation |
| GAP-009 | No temporal no-animation metric or universal low-variety metric is returned by `renderEffectFrame` | Shade `src/tools/browser/render.ts`; filter-only temporal/color logic is in `passthrough.ts` | no-animation requires an external two-frame comparison; low variety needs effect exemptions |
| GAP-010 | Uniform responsiveness returns overall `ok` when any tested uniform passes | Shade `src/tools/browser/uniforms.ts` — `testUniformResponsiveness()` | caller must inspect every `tested_uniforms` entry; outer status is unsafe |
| GAP-011 | Uniform responsiveness omits measured luma/channel deltas | Shade `src/tools/browser/uniforms.ts` — returned object | caller cannot audit the `>0.002` threshold from the report alone |
| GAP-012 | Upstream `renderEffectFrame` does not implement WebGPU pixel readback | Shade `src/tools/browser/render.ts` reads only `pipeline.backend.gl` | authoring can compile on WebGPU but single-backend frame metrics are unavailable there |
| GAP-013 | Noisemaker MCP config tracks an unpinned GitHub package reference | `.mcp.json` | tool schemas/thresholds can drift independently of this document snapshot |
| GAP-014 | A positive explicit `time` with warmup frames can pause animation before the awaited frame count and hang the tool | Shade `src/tools/browser/render.ts` pauses before its frame-count warmup promise; observed run reproduced the hang | a valid-looking render request can fail to terminate |
| GAP-015 | Similar metrics are computed by separate helpers with different sample grids/blank heuristics | Shade `src/tools/browser/render.ts`, `dsl.ts`, and `parity.ts`; Noisemaker `vendor/shade-mcp/harness/pixel-reader.js` | metric names cannot be assumed numerically interchangeable across verbs |
| GAP-016 | No preflight reports whether an effect is authorable/renderable on each backend before compilation | `shaders/src/renderer/canvas.js` — shader discovery; backend `compileProgram()` methods | impossible parity cases are found only by source/compile failure |
| GAP-017 | Shade's definition parser is regex-based and lossy | Shade `src/formats/definition-js.ts` — `parseDefinitionJs()` | list/analysis tools cannot expose the complete Noisemaker definition schema |
| GAP-018 | MCP selector inputs described as globs are compared literally | Shade `src/tools/resolve-effects.ts` — `resolveEffectIds()` | wildcard-looking selectors silently do not expand |
| GAP-019 | `testNoPassthrough` varies time/color rather than injecting and comparing an input texture | Shade `src/tools/browser/passthrough.ts` — `testNoPassthrough()` | its name overstates what is measured; true input passthrough is not tested |
| GAP-020 | MCP tools have no shared error envelope | Shade `src/tools/**` registration handlers return incompatible payload unions | transport success and `status:"ok"` cannot be handled uniformly |
| GAP-021 | `renderEffectFrame` requested resolution can differ from returned canvas dimensions without a warning | Shade `src/tools/browser/render.ts` sets viewport but reads canvas dimensions; exact transcript requested `256x256` and returned `90x90` | thresholds run on an unexpected sample size unless the caller checks `frame.width/height` |
| GAP-022 | Mutation introspection includes terminal `write` as a replaceable nonstarter and exposes no node-kind field | `shaders/src/lang/transform.js` — `listSteps()`/`findStepByIndex()`; current `test_transform.js` and `test_compiler_phase2.js` still expect effect-only lengths | callers can target a builtin accidentally and cannot distinguish it structurally without string matching |
| GAP-023 | `test_compiler_phase2.js` catches assertion failures without setting a nonzero process exit code | `shaders/tests/test_compiler_phase2.js` — `test()` catch only writes `console.error` | automation can report process success while the suite prints `FAIL`; callers must scan output until the harness is fixed |
| GAP-024 | `compileEffect`, `renderEffectFrame`, `describeEffectFrame`, `benchmarkEffectFPS`, `testUniformResponsiveness`, `testNoPassthrough`, and `runDslProgram` can accept the previous effect's ready status text before the selected effect finishes compiling | Shade `src/tools/browser/compile.ts`, `render.ts`, `benchmark.ts`, `uniforms.ts`, `passthrough.ts`, and `dsl.ts` poll status text; `describe.ts` delegates to `renderEffectFrame`; `parity.ts` explicitly replaces that pattern with `pipeline.isCompiling` readiness | an `ok` result, pass list, frame metric, vision report, or benchmark can describe a stale graph; require an independent graph/effect identity signal or use `testPixelParity`, which has the fixed readiness check |
| GAP-025 | `analyzeBranching` spreads unvalidated AI JSON after its computed `status` field | Shade `src/tools/analysis/branching.ts` — `return { status: ..., ...parsed }` | AI output can replace `ok`/`warning` with any JSON value; callers must validate the payload and recompute the opportunity count |
| GAP-026 | `onInit`, `onUpdate`, and `onDestroy` are copied into `Effect` wrappers but are not invoked by the production renderer | `shaders/src/runtime/effect.js` defines the wrappers; only `shaders/tests/harness_effect.js` calls them; `shaders/src/runtime/pipeline.js` calls `asyncInit` only | an authored config callback can look valid but never run in production; use only `asyncInit` as a production lifecycle hook until callers exist |

The matrix has 9 surfaces × 4 capabilities = 36 filled cells. Cells that rely
on behavior absent from code link to the 26 gaps above rather than substituting
an invented contract.
